steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'to call' into a simple word used when calling someone's name, like saying 'Hey!' in English
Makes the call stronger and longer, like saying 'Ohhh!'
Adds يا to call out to someone, like saying 'Hey!'
A formal way to say 'O you' - combines يا (O) with أيّ (which) and ها (this) to make a respectful call
The doubled ي (yaa) shows this is their special role or job
Adds definite article ال which causes the ن to get a shadda
Adds damma (ُ) to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he protected' in the past tense
Changed initial و to ا and added ت with shadda to show doing action to oneself

steps.m: protect yourself! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed to command form by removing the last vowel and changing middle vowel to kasra. The ا at start becomes ٱ in writing.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This is the unique name of God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda make it specific to the one true God. The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon in this sentence.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. This word negates the command تُطِعِ (obey) that comes after it.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it.
Dropped middle letter و and added vowels to make a command meaning 'obey!'
Adding تُ changes it from a command to a passive verb meaning 'to be obeyed'

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Forms active participle to describe someone who performs the action
Forms regular masculine plural and shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تُطِعِ (do not obey)
Makes the plural noun definite by adding the definite article ٱلْ and elongates the alif
Adds مُ at start and changes vowels to make it mean 'someone who pretends/acts fake'
Changes from singular 'hypocrite' to plural 'hypocrites' and shows they are receiving the action in 'do not obey the hypocrites'
Makes the plural noun definite
Adds coordinating conjunction prefix
This is an emphasis particle that makes the statement stronger. It's like adding 'truly' or 'definitely' at the start of a sentence in English. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah was wise.
Added shadda to show emphasis of the letter ل

steps.m: Allah (as the object being acted upon in تَّقِ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being feared/revered in the command 'fear Allah'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'

steps.m: he was/existed (describing Allah being عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا - All-Knowing, All-Wise)
steps.n: The middle weak letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before and after it in the past tense. This is a common pattern in hollow verbs.
This pattern shows someone who has complete and perfect knowledge. When used for Allah, it means He knows everything completely.

steps.m: All-Knowing (describing Allah in كَانَ عَلِيمًا - He was/is All-Knowing)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word completes the meaning of كَانَ (was/is) in this ayah, telling us about how Allah always was and is.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes the verb into an adjective showing someone who has a lot of wisdom

steps.m: very wise (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to make it describe Allah as being wise in this sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he followed' to 'follow!' by adding اِ at start - this makes it a command to one person
The ت and ب merged together with a shadda, making the command stronger - like saying 'follow closely!'

steps.m: and follow intensely! (command to follow what is being revealed to you)
steps.n: Added 'and' (و) at the start of the word
This word connects 'is revealed' (يُوحَىٰٓ) with 'to you' (إِلَيْكَ), showing what is being revealed
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: is being revealed (to إِلَيْكَ - to you)
steps.n: Changed to passive present tense to show ongoing revelation being received. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb ending in ي.
The small alif (ٰٓ) helps show the correct pronunciation of the ى
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for the attached pronoun
Added كَ at the end to show this is directed 'to you'. This word connects to يُوحَىٰٓ (is revealed) in the ayah, showing what is being revealed to.

steps.m: from (connects رَّبِّكَ to what comes before it)
steps.n: A simple preposition showing that what follows (your Lord) is the source or origin of what came before (what is revealed). In this ayah, it connects 'what is revealed' to 'your Lord' by showing that the revelation comes from Him.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and develop something to completion
master, one who nurtures to completion
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who completely masters the act of nurturing and developing

steps.m: master, one who nurtures to completion (emphasized)
steps.n: Adding shadda at the beginning emphasizes the word since it comes after a word ending in a vowel (مِن)
Adding kasra shows this word is possessed by or connected to something that follows
Adding كَ shows this Lord belongs to you (the one being addressed). This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle
Added kasra under إ and shadda on ن to make it stronger. This word emphasizes that what comes after it (Allah) is definitely true.
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it the proper name for The One God

Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. This word is what is being followed in the command 'follow' (اتَّبِعْ).

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he/it was' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This word connects to خَبِيرًا (All-Aware) in this ayah, showing that Allah was and continues to be All-Aware.
This is the basic relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'
Added the preposition بِ (with) to show how this connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you do/work). It means 'with what you do'

steps.m: to do, to act, to work

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he did) to present tense (he does)
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to inform, to be aware of details

steps.n: Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
Changes from a verb (informing) to an adjective describing someone who is knowledgeable
The tanween alif shows this is describing Allah in this context, functioning as a predicate after كَانَ (was)
Adding fatha vowels creates basic past tense verb form
Adding تَ at beginning and doubling middle letter shows doing action for oneself

steps.m: put your trust in! (command to one person)
steps.n: Removing the last vowel (a) and adding a stop mark (sukun) changes it from 'he trusted' to 'you trust!' (command)

steps.m: and put your trust in! (command to one person)
steps.n: Adding وَ at beginning connects this command to what came before it
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, depending on, or directed towards something else. Here it connects 'rely' (تَوَكَّلْ) with 'Allah' (ٱللَّهِ) to mean 'rely upon Allah'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word بِ (by/with). The alif at the start changed to a special connecting alif (ٱ). The doubled lam is shown with a shadda.

steps.m: to be enough or sufficient for something

steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. The ي at the end becomes ى because it's at the end of a word.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is the proper name of God in Islam, derived from the root meaning divine/deity but used exclusively for the One God

Added بِ at beginning to show how to rely on Allah, and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb توكل (rely/trust). This word is receiving the action of trusting/relying in the phrase 'and rely upon Allah'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who does this action as their role
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of كَفَىٰ (sufficient) in the phrase 'sufficient is Allah as guardian'
This is the basic negative particle 'ma' that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates 'جَعَلَ' (made/placed).
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the negation, making the statement stronger. It's like saying 'definitely not' or 'absolutely not' in English.

steps.m: to make or put something in a certain state/position

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word relates to what Allah says (يَقُولُ) in this verse.
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'
Added the u-mark at the end to show this is the subject

steps.m: to be a man, to walk

steps.n: The vowels show this is a noun meaning 'a man'
Added the two dots below (tanween kasra) to show this is 'a man' when something is happening to him
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for/to'. This word is connected to the verb جَعَلَ (He made/placed) in this ayah.
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of. Here it connects to قَلْبَيْنِ (two hearts) to show what Allah did not give.
Added shadda (doubling mark) on م because it comes after another م in the previous word (ما). This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
The root relates to changing or turning something
The root meaning of turning becomes 'heart' because the heart is always turning and pumping. This is the basic word for heart.
Adding َيْنِ at the end shows we're talking about exactly two hearts. This word is part of a larger phrase saying 'Allah did not give a man two hearts.'
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the location of قَلْبَيْنِ (two hearts) being inside جَوْفِهِ (his chest/interior).

steps.m: to be hollow or have space inside
steps.n: The root describes something that has space inside it
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'inside' or 'cavity'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِۦ to show this belongs to 'him' (the man mentioned)
This is a basic negative/interrogative particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb جَعَلَ (made/appointed).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is being used in relation to يَقُولُ (he says) in this ayah.

steps.m: to join things together as pairs

steps.n: Changed the word into a plural noun showing multiple pairs or spouses
Added fatha to show word is plural form of زَوْج (spouse/pair)
Added كُمُ at the end to show these are 'your' spouses (speaking to a group)
Extended the alif to show plurality
The word 'who' gets 'the' (ال) added at its start and a special mark (hamza) at its end to make it mean 'those who'. It's talking about the wives in this verse.
Added alif after first letter to show doing something with someone else (like declaring something forbidden together)
Added تُ at start to make it 'you' and changed vowels to make it happening now instead of in the past
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) for Quranic spelling.
Added sukun to show the end of the preposition
Added the basic pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who/what is being referred to
Added نَّ to change the pronoun from singular masculine to plural feminine

steps.m: to be a mother, to lead as a mother does

steps.n: Root letters combined with 'u' sound to make the noun 'mother'
Changes from 'mother' to 'mothers' by adding ات at the end. The ه is added between أُمّ and ات in this special plural form.
Adds كُمْ to show the mothers belong to multiple people being addressed
This is a relative pronoun that connects to جَعَلَ (made) in this ayah

steps.m: and what/that which (relative pronoun connecting to جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement. This word connects to جَعَلَ (made) in the ayah.
This root carries the basic idea of making something into something else or putting something somewhere
Added fathas to show this is a past tense verb. In this ayah, Allah is the one who 'made' or 'established' something.
Changed root 'call' into plural noun 'adopted ones' by adding أ at start and ياء + اء at end
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of جَعَلَ (made/set), and elongated the alif
Added كُمْ to show these belong to the people being addressed
The root letters combine to form the basic word for 'son', which comes from the idea of building/establishing a family
Changes from 'son' to 'sons' by making a broken plural - adds أَ at start and stretches middle with اء
The fatha (َ) shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلَ (made/appointed). The alif is lengthened to آ for emphasis.
Adding كُمْ shows this belongs to multiple people being spoken to (you all)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this'
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance, changing 'this' to 'that'
Adding لِ strengthens the meaning of distance
Adding كُمْ shows this is addressing multiple people
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the noun form meaning 'saying'
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added كُم to show this belongs to multiple people being addressed
This is an irregular plural form for body parts
Changes to broken plural form to show multiple mouths/openings
Added pronoun كُمْ to show possession by multiple people
Added preposition ب to show how something is done
This is the proper name of God in Islam. It comes from the word for deity (ilah) but is a unique name that can only refer to the one true God.
Added shadda on ل to show it is pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (saying) in this sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Changed the alif to a waṣla form since it connects with the و before it in pronunciation
This is the basic meaning of these three letters together
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now, not in the past
The middle root letter gets a dhamma (ُ) which is common for hollow verbs
The second letter is doubled (has shadda) to make it stronger and more emphatic
Adding the fatha (َ) shows this is a past tense verb
Added ال (with hamzat wasl ٱ) to make it specific. This shows we're talking about absolute truth.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يَقُولُ (he says) in this ayah. Allah is saying the truth.
This is a basic word that means 'he'. It's used to refer to Allah in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This shows that Allah both speaks the truth (previous part) AND guides to the way (next part).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start. The ى changes to ي because it's in the middle of a sentence. This word is connected to السَّبِيلَ (the way) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to make a path or way
steps.n: This root relates to creating or following paths
Adding ي and vowels changes this from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: the path/way (object of يَهْدِى - he guides)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِى (he guides)
The root letters were connected with vowels (َ) and the last letter و changed to ا to make 'he called' in the past tense
Changed to command form by adding اُ at start and putting ُ after ع. The ا at the end drops off in commands.
Added و at the end to make it specifically for commanding one male
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being called
Root letters أ-ب-و become the simple word for 'father'
Changes from 'father' to 'fathers' by stretching the first letter and adding 'aa' at the end
Adds preposition لِ meaning 'to/for'
Changes ending to allow possession
Adds possessive pronoun 'their'
This is a standalone pronoun with no root. It's one of the basic building blocks of Arabic that shows who or what we're talking about.
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly: هُ (hu) وَ (wa)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

steps.m: more/most just (than calling them by other than their fathers' names)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is more/most just than something else. In this ayah, it's saying that calling adopted children by their real fathers' names is more just than calling them by other names.
This is a preposition that shows location or presence. While it has letters that look like a root, it's treated as a basic word.
Added vowel marks to make it work as a preposition. In this ayah, it connects with اللَّهِ (Allah) to show something is 'with/near Allah'.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to what comes before)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and shadda on ل to emphasize the special nature of this name. The hamza at the start becomes a wasl form (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if you do not know their fathers'

steps.m: and/then if (connects this condition to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about calling people by their fathers' names
This is a negative word that makes the verb after it (تَعْلَمُوٓا۟ - you know) into a past negative (you did not know)
Connected the root letters together
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action, and added vowel marks to show it's happening now
Added وا at the end to show many people are doing the action together
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for 'father'
Plural form of 'father' - changes the middle of the word and adds a long sound at the end
Adds هُمْ at the end to show possession - 'their fathers'
Connected the root letters together to form base word
Changed from 'brother' to 'brothers' using a special plural form (إِخْوَان) that's common for family words
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite - meaning 'some brothers' rather than 'the brothers'
Added كُمْ to show possession - 'your brothers'
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence
Basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to الدِّينِ (the religion) to show 'in the religion'.

steps.m: to follow a way or custom

steps.n: The kasra vowel transforms the root into a noun denoting an abstract concept
The first letter gets doubled (shown by ّ) because of the upcoming ال

steps.m: the religion (in فِى ٱلدِّينِ - in the religion)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after في (in/concerning)
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic word for one who protects or is close
Added مَ at the start and changed the vowels to make a noun meaning 'protector' - like changing 'protect' to 'protector'
Makes the word plural by adding ا after و and changing the ending to ي - like changing 'protector' to 'protectors'
Added و at start to mean 'and'
Added كُمْ at the end to show it means 'your' (plural)
This is a special verb used for negation. Unlike most Arabic words, it doesn't follow normal root patterns.
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'is not' - it's like saying 'there is no' before something. Here it's saying 'there is no blame'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before attached pronouns to maintain proper pronunciation
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Changed from a root meaning 'to lean towards wrong' into a noun meaning 'wrongdoing' by adding the vowel mark ُ (damma)
Added ا (alif) in the middle to make it mean 'blame' instead of 'doing wrong'
Added tanween dammah (double damma) to show it's indefinite and in the nominative case
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ما which makes the phrase ask about or refer to something general
The alif is lengthened for emphasis and to show the end of the word clearly when reading
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added أ at the start to make it mean 'to cause/make a mistake' instead of just 'to mistake'
Added تُ suffix to show first person past tense
Changed تُ (meaning I) to تُم (meaning you all) to show many people did the action
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or what is used

steps.m: with it (referring to what was done by mistake in أَخْطَأْتُم)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what the preposition connects to. The small ۦ after هِ is just a pronunciation mark.
Basic adversative particle that shows contrast with what came before

steps.m: and yet/but still (connects to previous statement فَإِن لَّمْ تَعْلَمُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this contrasting idea to the previous statement about not knowing their fathers
Basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'what'
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This emphasis shows up because the word comes after في to form فيما (in what)

steps.m: to intend or do something purposefully
steps.n: This is the basic meaning showing the idea of doing something on purpose
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show this was done very purposefully
Added تْ at the end to show a female did this action
Connected the root letters together to form the singular noun for 'heart'
Makes it plural to mean multiple hearts
Adds second person masculine plural possessive pronoun
Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in past tense

steps.m: and he was (connects to غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا - Most Forgiving, Most Merciful)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: to worship, to deify, to be devoted to
steps.n: This root carries the basic meaning of worship and devotion
Root meaning 'worship' became a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
Added ال to specify this is The One God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added the shadda to show doubled lam, changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects with previous word, and added damma to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was)
This is the basic concept of forgiveness or covering up mistakes
Added vowels and و to show this describes someone who forgives a lot

steps.m: very forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing Allah in this sentence
Intensive form meaning 'most merciful'
Added vowels and ي to make it mean 'one who shows a lot of mercy'
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning even more
Added tanween (double line ending) to show this describes someone/something in the sentence
Root meaning 'news' becomes 'someone who brings news from God' by adding ي. Like changing 'message' to 'messenger'
Adding vowel marks to show it's a noun describing someone who does something
Adding ّ makes the last letter stronger to show importance
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action أَوْلَىٰ (is closer/more worthy)
Added vowels (َ ِ َ) to make it mean 'he was close to' in the past
Changed to comparative form to show the Prophet is closer/more worthy than others. The ى at the end is called 'alif maqsurah' and is just a different way to write the long a sound.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long a sound. This doesn't change the meaning.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling secure
Changed the word to show who does the action instead of the action itself
Added ين to show we're talking about many believers
Added بِ at the start to mean 'with/by' and ٱلْ to mean 'the'. This word is connected to أَوْلَىٰ (more worthy) in the ayah.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects the word أَوْلَىٰ (closer/more worthy) with ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the believers), showing that the Prophet is closer to the believers than they are to themselves.
Added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to make it plural, like changing 'self' to 'selves'

steps.m: their selves (referring to المؤمنين - the believers)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show it belongs to them (the believers mentioned earlier in the verse)
Root letters formed into noun meaning one spouse/partner
Changed from single spouse to multiple spouses by adding أ at start and ا in middle
Added coordinating conjunction 'and'
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action

steps.m: and his spouses (referring to the Prophet's wives who are mothers to the believers)
steps.n: Added possessive pronoun 'his' referring to the Prophet ﷺ. The long alif is written as ٰ for style.

steps.m: to be a mother, to lead as a mother does

steps.n: The ُ (damma) sound makes it into the noun 'mother'

steps.m: their mothers (referring to the Prophet's wives being like mothers to the believers)
steps.n: The word for 'mother' (أُمّ) became plural (أُمَّهٰت) and هُمْ was added at the end to mean 'their'. The ُ shows it's doing the action in the sentence.
This is a special word form that shows ownership or possession. The و at the end shows it's talking about multiple people.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and the possessors of (connected to الْأَرْحَامِ - blood relatives)
steps.n: Added small alif marks (۟) to show proper pronunciation and added ا at the end as a writing variant. This word connects to الْأَرْحَامِ (blood relatives) to mean 'and those who possess blood relations'

steps.m: to show mercy, to care for family

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'womb' - shows connection between mercy and family
Changed to plural by adding أَ at start and ا after م to make أَرْحَام (like how book/كتاب becomes books/أكتاب)

steps.m: the blood relatives (connected to أُو۟لُوا۟ - those who have)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (the) at start and ِ at end to show this refers to specific blood relatives mentioned in the verse. This word is connected to أُو۟لُوا۟ (those who have) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word follows أولى (closer/more entitled to) in the ayah.
The word gets a damma (u sound) at the end because it's doing the action
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to a group being talked about

steps.m: to be close to or take care of something
he was close to/took care of
steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to be close to'

steps.m: more worthy/closer (to the believers than themselves)
steps.n: Changed to comparative form to show 'more' of the quality. This word relates to المؤمنين (the believers) in this ayah, showing the Prophet's special closeness to them.

steps.m: more worthy/closer (to the believers than themselves)

steps.m: he promised (said by المنافقون والذين في قلوبهم مرض)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb. This word shows what the hypocrites and those with disease in their hearts were saying.

steps.m: he promised us (said by المنافقون والذين في قلوبهم مرض)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show who received the promise (us). This refers to what Allah and His Messenger promised the hypocrites and those with disease in their hearts.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of وَعَدَنَا (promised us) in this ayah.
Changes the verb 'to send' into 'one who is sent' using the pattern فَعُول which shows someone's main role
Adds و to connect this word with الله (Allah) mentioned earlier
Adds ُ to show this word is a subject together with الله

steps.m: and His messenger (doer, joined to الله)
steps.n: Adds هُ meaning 'His' to show this is Allah's messenger. The ۥٓ marks are pronunciation helpers.
This is a basic exception particle that shows the promise mentioned was nothing but deception. It connects directly to وَعَدَنَا (promised us) to limit or restrict its meaning.
Added damma vowels (ُ) and و to change from an action into a noun meaning the act of deceiving

steps.m: nothing but deception (what Allah and His Messenger promised, according to the hypocrites)
steps.n: Added ًا (an) at the end to make it 'a deception' - this shows it's the object being talked about in the sentence
This is a compound particle made of 'wa' (and) + 'idh' (when)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense form
The middle weak letter و changes to ا according to Arabic rules for hollow verbs
Added ت at the end to show a female is speaking. This word connects to 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) later in the verse.

steps.m: to move around something in a circle

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding a fat-ha (َ) and sukoon (ْ) to mean 'one complete circle'
Changed the middle و to ا and added ِ to show who does the action
Adding ة makes it refer to a group rather than an individual
Adding tanween makes it 'a' group instead of 'the' group
The basic preposition meaning 'from'
Shadda added to initial meem because it comes after و (and) in the ayah, which causes this sound change

steps.m: from them (referring to the group mentioned as طَّآئِفَةٌ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this refers to 'them' - specifically referring back to the group (طَّآئِفَةٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning 'people/family'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon
Added يٰٓ at the beginning to show we're calling out to these people. This word is connected to يَثْرِبَ (Yathrib) which comes right after it.
This is a proper noun naming the city that later became known as Medina

steps.m: Yathrib (object of يٰٓأَهْلَ - O people of)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and fatha at end to show this is the object being addressed. This connects to يٰٓأَهْلَ (O people of) right before it.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates مُقَامَ (place/position), saying there is no place/position for them.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to stand or stay in a place
steps.n: The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
The root letters were connected and vowels added. The middle letter و changed to ا to make it easier to say - this is common in Arabic verbs.
Added مُ at the beginning to change the meaning from the action of staying to the place where staying happens
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added كُمْ which shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to
This shows the basic concept of returning or going back
Changed the root into a command by adding marks (sukun and kasra) - like saying 'go back!'
Added alif at start to make it a proper command form
Added وا at the end to show this command is for a group
Added فَ at start to connect this command with what came before it
This pattern creates a noun from the root
Root verb 'to permit' changed into the noun 'permission'
Added letters to show seeking or requesting permission
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous phrase

steps.m: to separate or split things apart
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of splitting or separating things
Changed from basic root 'split' to show someone/something that is already split apart by adding vowel marks
Added ي to make it mean 'a group' instead of just the action of separating

steps.m: a group (that is separate from others)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is talking about any group, not a specific one
Starting with the basic preposition that means 'from'
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to
Two pronunciation changes happen: 1) The ن gets a shadda (ّ) because it comes after ال in the previous word, and 2) The final م gets a ُ because the next word starts with ال
Basic three-letter root form representing the core meaning
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the word mean 'news/information' (this is a noun, not a verb)
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added hamza at the end to show who does the action
Changed hamza to shadda to show this is someone who regularly and importantly brings divine news
Added ال at the beginning to point to a specific prophet, and the first letter gets a shadda because it comes after ال
The word 'the Prophet' gets a special mark at the end to show someone is asking his permission
Added vowels to connect the root letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action. The middle ا returns to و.
Added ونَ at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action. This word appears in the ayah when a group of people are making excuses to leave.
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to help with pronunciation
Added shadda (ّ) to make the meaning stronger. This word emphasizes what they are saying about their houses.
Changed the root into a noun meaning the place where the action happens
Changed from singular to plural by changing the middle vowel and adding و - this is a common pattern for making plurals
Added a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end because the word إِنَّ came before it and makes it the thing being talked about
Added نَا at the end to show the houses belong to us
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters. The و gets a sukun to show it's a long vowel.

steps.m: a specific vulnerable spot or weakness
steps.n: Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of vulnerability
Added tanween dammah to make it indefinite in the nominative case
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of the negative particle ما

steps.m: and not (houses of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates their claim about their houses being exposed, connecting to how 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) earlier in the verse.
This is a feminine pronoun used to refer back to بُيُوتَنَا (our homes) in the previous part of the verse. It's feminine because بُيُوت (homes) is treated as feminine in Arabic.
This is the basic root meaning
Added vowels to make root letters into a simple noun meaning 'weakness/exposure'
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance
Added بِ to show reason or cause

steps.m: due to being exposed/vulnerable (in relation to يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is part of what they're saying

steps.m: if (introduces a condition in يَقُولُونَ they say)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it's part of what they are saying (يَقُولُونَ)
This is the basic root meaning
Added vowels to the root letters to make 'he wanted' in the past tense
Added present tense prefix يُ and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

This is a basic particle that shows an exception - it tells us that what comes after it is excluded from what was mentioned before. Here it connects to يُرِيدُونَ (they want) to show that the only thing they want is what comes next (escape).
This is the basic root meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word from a verb (action) to a noun (thing/concept)

steps.m: running away (as the object being discussed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of this conditional particle
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This introduces a hypothetical scenario connected to the verb دُخِلَتْ (it was entered)

steps.m: was entered (passive form showing cities were entered)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that something was done to the cities, rather than the cities doing something themselves

steps.m: it was entered (referring to the cities)
steps.n: Added ت at the end because we're talking about cities, which are treated as feminine in Arabic
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' - it comes from the idea of being high or elevated above something
The ى changes to يْ when we're going to add something to the end of the word
Added هِم at the end to show who is being acted upon (them)
This is a primitive preposition that has no root or derivational steps. The vowels are part of the base word.
Added shadda because this word comes after a vowel at the end of the previous word. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from a verb (doing something) to a noun by changing the vowels - now means a specific area or direction
Added أَ at start and ا after second root letter to make plural
Added ِهَا to show this belongs to something feminine. The ِ (kasra) appears because this word follows مِنْ (from)

steps.m: then (connecting to سُئِلُوا - they would be asked)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. Here it connects what happens next (being asked to join the enemy) with what was mentioned before (the enemy entering from all sides).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone received the action instead of doing it

steps.m: they were asked (referring to a group of people)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that many people received this action
Changed from action of testing to the trial itself. The ة makes it refer to one specific instance of trial.

steps.m: trial/test (object of سُئِلُوا - they were asked)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon - it's what they were asked about in the ayah

steps.m: the trial/test (object of سُئِلُوا - they were asked)
steps.n: Added ال to show this refers to a specific trial - the one being discussed in this context about attacking Medina
Added لَ at beginning to mean 'surely/definitely' + changed verb form to 'would give/bring'
Added و to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they would surely give/bring it (feminine object)
steps.n: Added هَا to show what is being given/brought (referring back to الْفِتْنَةَ - the trial/persecution)
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates تَلَبَّثُوا۟ (they remained) which comes after it.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a basic past tense verb
The ت at the start and doubled middle letter show someone doing this to themselves, making it mean hesitate rather than just stay
The suffix وا changes the subject from singular to plural masculine
Basic pronoun form referring back to الْفِتْنَةَ (the trial/persecution) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: with it (referring to الْفِتْنَةَ - the trial/persecution)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'with' something. The آ at the end is just a writing style for هَا when it comes after بِ.

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a standalone particle used for exception. In this ayah, it shows that the time period mentioned after it (يَسِيرًا - a little while) is excluded from the previous statement about how long they would stay.
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ي to change from 'being easy' to 'easy' as a describing word (like light/heavy/big)
Added ًا (tanween) at the end to make it an adverb - changes from 'easy' to 'easily'
This is the base particle that shows certainty about what follows
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Adding the fatha (َ) shows this is a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is connected to عٰهَدُوا۟ (they made a covenant) in this ayah.

steps.m: to promise or make an agreement

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding ا after first letter makes it mean doing something with someone else
Added وا with damma before it to show many people did this action. This word comes after كَانُوا (they were) in the ayah.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of عٰهَدُوا۟ - they made a covenant with)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence. They made a covenant with Allah. Also added shadda to emphasize the L sound and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.

steps.m: from (connects to قَبْلُ to mean 'from before')
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it combines with قَبْلُ (before) to show when something happened in the past.

steps.m: to come before or face towards

steps.n: Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'before'
Adds damma to show this word is in nominative case
This is a negative word that makes the verb after it (يُوَلُّونَ - they turn) into its opposite meaning. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English (e.g., 'not turning').
Added shadda to show intensity of action and fatha for past tense
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to كَانُوا (they were) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a noun meaning 'back' or 'rear'
Changed to plural by adding 'a' at start and 'aa' after 'd' - like saying 'back' becomes 'backs'

steps.m: the backs (object of يُوَلُّونَ - they turn)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and fatha at end to show it's the object being turned. This is what they are turning in the phrase 'they turn the backs' (meaning they retreat/flee)
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas

steps.m: and he was (connected to عَهْدُ which comes after)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes before it. This word connects to عَهْدُ (promise) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to promise or make an agreement

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks. This word is connected to الله to show it's Allah's promise.
Added ُ at the end to show this word owns something or is connected to what comes after it (الله)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to elongate the sound
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
The word 'Allah' comes from 'al-ilah' (the god), and here it has a kasra (-i) sound at the end because it's being promised to
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'asked' to 'being asked' by adding ma- at start and changing middle vowels - makes it about receiving the action
Adds tanween alif (ـًا) to show this word is receiving the action and is indefinite
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed to a command form by adding damma on first letter and dropping the weak middle letter و. This word gives the command that introduces what follows in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that negates future actions. It has no root or derivational steps as it is a fixed form particle (حرف).
Adding the shadda (ّ) on ل makes the negation stronger. This word connects to يَنفَعَكُمُ (benefit you) to say it will absolutely never happen.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future, and changed the last vowel to ُ
Added كُمُ at the end to show the benefit is going to multiple people being spoken to (you all)
Changed the root into a noun that means the action itself. This word is the subject of يَنفَعَ (will benefit) in لَّن يَنفَعَكُمُ (will never benefit you).
Added ال (the) to make it specific and ُ (damma) to show it's doing the action in the sentence. This is the subject of يَنفَعَ (will benefit) in the phrase لَّن يَنفَعَكُمُ (will never benefit you).
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'fleeing' with what would happen as a result.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added sukun on the last letter to prepare it for attachments

steps.m: you all ran away (referring to a group being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added تُم at the end to show that many people are being talked to directly
This is a preposition that shows something coming from or originating somewhere
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word الْمَوْتِ (death). Together they mean 'from death'
Adding the vowel marks changes this from a root into a proper noun meaning 'death'
Added ال to make it specific - not just any death, but the death that everyone must face
This is a basic connecting word that gives us a choice between two things, like saying "this or that" in English
Added ِ at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْقَتْلِ (death). This shows they go together in the sentence.
Changed the verb into a noun that means the action itself
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes the meaning from just 'then' to 'in that case'

steps.m: and in that case (connects to لَّا تُمَتَّعُونَ - you will not be given enjoyment)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this phrase to what follows, showing the result of the previous statement about fleeing
This is a basic negative particle that shows something is not true or should not happen
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger. Here it emphasizes that fleeing will absolutely not benefit them.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'
Adding تُ at start makes it passive - someone else caused the enjoyment
Added ending that shows this applies to a group of people
This is a basic particle that doesn't derive from a root. It's used to exclude something from what was mentioned before.
Added shadda and alif at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it's excluding 'a little' (قَلِيلًا) from the enjoyment mentioned in تُمَتَّعُونَ (you will be allowed to enjoy).

steps.m: to be small in amount or number

steps.n: Adds vowels to show this describes a temporary quality of being small/few
Added ي to make it an adjective that describes how little something is

steps.m: for a little while (describes how long they will enjoy)
steps.n: Added ًا to make it an adverb - changes from 'little' to 'for a little while', showing how long something lasts (connects to تُمَتَّعُونَ/enjoyment in the verse)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the weak middle letter و between the vowels. This word starts a sentence where Allah tells Prophet Muhammad ﷺ what to say.

steps.m: who (asking about the person that يَعْصِمُكُم protects you)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about people. In this ayah, it's asking who could protect them, connecting to the verb يَعْصِمُكُم (protects you) that comes after it.
This is a demonstrative word that points to something. Here it combines with ٱلَّذِى after it to mean 'who' or 'the one who', asking about a person.
This is the base demonstrative form that points to something
Adding ٱلّ changes this from pointing at something to connecting parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'who' to the verb 'protects you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he protects you all (from مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ - from Allah)
steps.n: Added 'كُم' at the end to mean 'you all' as the ones being protected, and connects with 'from Allah' to show who they need protection from
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else
The م is doubled (has a shadda) to show emphasis in pronunciation because it comes after a connecting word
The fatha (َ) is added at the end to connect this word smoothly to the next word (اللَّهِ)
Added shadda to emphasize the L sound
Added kasra at the end to show possession. In this ayah, this word shows what دُونِ (besides/other than) is referring to.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - if something happens, then something else follows. Here it introduces the condition 'if He intends' (أَرَادَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at start makes it mean 'to want/intend something specific'
Added kasra to the preposition ب to make it readable

steps.m: with you all (object of أَرَادَ - he intended)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كُم which means 'you all'. This word is the object of أَرَادَ (he intended) in this ayah, showing who is being intended.
Changed from a root meaning 'to harm' into a noun meaning 'evil' by adding a damma (ُ) sound

steps.m: an evil/harm (object of أَرَادَ - he intended)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to make it a direct object (the thing being wanted). The long alif (ٓ) helps show how to say it correctly.

steps.m: or (connecting أَرَادَ بِكُمْ سُوٓءًا with أَرَادَ بِكُمْ رَحْمَةً)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows two possibilities: either Allah intends harm for you OR mercy. In this ayah, it connects two parallel phrases about what Allah might intend.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start changes the meaning to 'wanting' something specific
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words. In this ayah, this verb connects to بِكُمْ which shows who is being affected.
Added kasra to the letter ب to make it readable
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This word is the object of أَرَادَ (he intended/wanted) in this ayah.
Root letters ر-ح-م were joined with vowels to form رَحْم meaning 'mercy' as a simple noun
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of mercy
Added tanween to show this is the object receiving the action in the sentence
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find), making it mean 'they do not find'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added يَ at beginning to show it's happening now, and added vowels ِ and ُ to connect the letters

steps.m: they find (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show many people are doing the action. This refers back to the disbelievers being discussed in this part of the ayah.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for them (referring to those who try to protect others from Allah)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who the action is for. This connects to the earlier part of the verse talking about those who claim they can protect others from Allah.
Basic preposition showing separation or starting point
The shadda appears because this word is followed by the word اللَّهِ which starts with ال. When مِن comes before ال, the ن sound blends into the ل sound, shown by the shadda.

steps.m: to be below or less than something
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of being lower, either in position, rank, or quality
Adding the damma (ُ) changes it from a root to a word meaning 'below'
The kasra at the end connects this word to the word Allah after it, showing that something is besides/other than Allah
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam
Added ِ at the end to show this is the object of مِنَ (from). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to protect or be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb showing someone protected or was close to something
Changes from a verb (to protect) into a noun meaning someone who protects others

steps.m: a protector/guardian (as object of يَجِدُونَ - they find)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find)
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find) in this ayah.

steps.m: one who helps a lot (like helper or supporter)
steps.n: Added ي with kasra (ِ) to show this is someone who helps a lot, like turning 'help' into 'helper' in English

steps.m: a helper/supporter (object of يَجِدُونَ - they find)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of finding - they don't find any helper
When قَدْ comes before a present tense verb like يَعْلَمُ (knows), it adds emphasis to show that something definitely happens or is happening. Here it emphasizes that Allah definitely knows.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, sukun on ع to help pronunciation, and ُ at end to show this is the subject doing the action. This word refers to Allah (اللَّهُ) in this ayah.
Changes the action into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Adds the nominative case ending to show this word is a subject
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Added مُ at start and doubled middle letter to show someone who does this action a lot
Forms regular masculine plural

steps.m: the specific people who prevent others (subject of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added ال to point to specific preventers that Allah knows about in this verse

steps.m: the specific people who prevent others (object of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end to show these people are the object (the ones being acted upon) in this verse
Combines 'from' (مِن) with 'you all' (كُمْ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter. This pattern shows who does the action.
Added ال to make it specific
Added ين to show it's talking about many people

steps.m: and the ones who speak/say (object of يَعْلَمُ/knows)
steps.n: Added و (and) at the beginning to connect it to the previous phrase. The word is being acted upon by the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows) in this ayah.
Root meaning 'brother' was changed to make many brothers by adding ان at the end
Added لِ (li-) at beginning to mean 'for/to the brothers'
Added هم (their) at the end to show these are 'their brothers'
This is a command word telling someone to come. It's actually made up of two parts that merged long ago: هَا (here) + لُمَّ (gather)
Added vowels to make it a command form. This word comes from combining هَا (here) with لُمَّ (gather).
Adding shadda makes the command more emphatic
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
Added sukun (ْ) and changed ى to ي to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added نَا at the end which means 'us' or 'our', showing who is being moved towards
Starting with the basic negation word that means 'not'

steps.m: and not (they do not come to battle)

Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. The ي at the end drops because it's a weak letter meeting another ending.

This is a basic particle that shows an exception. In this ayah, it shows that they only come to battle a little. It's connected to the verb يَأْتُونَ (they come).

Added ي to show it's a quality or characteristic

Changes from singular (one stingy person) to plural (many stingy people)

Basic meaning of seeing or perceiving with eyes or mind

The ى changes back to يْ when adding more letters after it
Added تَ to show 'you' (masculine) did the action
Added هُمْ to show who was seen (them)

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

Added the pronoun كَ which means 'you' to show who is being addressed

Added تَ to show a female/neutral subject doing the action
Added damma at end to show this is happening now

The base word ذا (this/that) becomes ذي when used as a relative pronoun (who/which)
Adding ال (the) turns 'who/which' into 'the one who' - making it point to something specific
Adding ك at the beginning makes it comparative, showing similarity. The alif gets a special mark (ٱ) because it comes after ك
Added basic past tense vowels. The و changes to ا at the end of words.

Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word الْمَوْتِ (death). Together they mean 'from death'.

Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word مِنَ (from) that comes before it in the ayah

This is a common word used to indicate when something happens. It often connects two events in time.

Changed from verb to noun by adding vowel patterns

Added -in sound at the end to show it's describing tongues (ألسنة)

Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun

steps.m: those (referring to the hypocrites mentioned before)

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

Added ًا at the end to show this describes how something is for Allah, connecting to كَانَ ذٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ (that was for Allah)

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
