steps.m: they made a promise/covenant (subject of صَدَقُوا۟)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to صَدَقُوا۟ (they were true) in describing the believers.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object of the verb 'they made a covenant with' (عٰهَدُوا)
This is a preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix

steps.m: upon him (referring to what they promised اللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this means 'upon him'. This refers back to the promise they made to Allah mentioned earlier in the verse.
Starting with the base preposition من
Added هُم at the end to show this refers to a group of men or mixed group
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about those who were truthful
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people
Added shadda on م for emphasis. This word is connected to يَنتَظِرُ (waits/is waiting) in this ayah, showing who is doing the waiting.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense. This word describes what the person (مَن) did.
The ى at the end is common for past tense verbs ending in a weak letter
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long aa sound

steps.m: to make a vow or pledge

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding specific vowel patterns. This word is what was fulfilled/completed in the verb قضى (he fulfilled).
Added هُ at the end to show the vow belongs to him. The small و (ۥ) is just to help with pronunciation.
Starting with the basic preposition مِنْ which shows something coming from somewhere or someone
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added هُم at the end to show this means 'from them' (referring back to the believers mentioned earlier)
This is a basic relative pronoun used to refer to people. It connects to the verb قَضَىٰ (he fulfilled) to show who did the action.
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound. This happens because the word comes after فَمِنْهُم in the ayah.
This is the basic meaning of looking or watching
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added ن and ت after first letter to show doing the action of waiting with purpose and expectation
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding ُ at end
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions/statements) or 'not' (for negation). In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's describing how they did not change.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. Here it means 'and not' because it's describing how they stayed faithful and did not change their commitment.
This is the basic meaning of changing something
The doubled middle letter (ّ) shows this action is done with intensity or multiple times. In this ayah, it refers to those who did not change their covenant with Allah.

steps.m: they changed/substituted (referring to those who stayed true to their covenant)
steps.n: The suffix وا was added to show that a group of men (3 or more) did this action in the past. In this ayah, it refers to those who did NOT change their commitment, so the meaning is actually opposite - it's talking about the faithful ones who stayed true.

steps.m: to exchange or replace one thing with another
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of changing something
Adding تَ at the start and ي in the middle turns this into a noun meaning 'the process of doing the action'. Like how 'teach' becomes 'teaching' in English.

steps.m: any change/alteration (object of بَدَّلُوا - they altered)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action. In this ayah, it emphasizes that they did not make ANY alteration to their commitment to Allah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لِ to show purpose ('in order to') and َ at end to show subjunctive mood

steps.m: so that he may reward (subject: اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The ل got stronger (shadda) and ي changed to ى at the end. Allah is doing the rewarding in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added damma to show this is the subject
Changes from verb 'to tell truth' to noun meaning 'one who tells truth'

steps.m: people who truly tell the truth
steps.n: Made plural to refer to multiple truthful people. This word is the object (the ones receiving the action) of the verb يجزي which means 'to reward'.

steps.m: the people who truly tell the truth
steps.n: Makes the noun definite by adding the definite article

steps.m: the people who truly tell the truth (who Allah will reward)
steps.n: The small mark at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word
Added هِمْ to show it belongs to them

steps.m: because of their truthfulness (reason for يَجْزِىَ/reward)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show this is the reason Allah will reward them
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more intense
Adding يُ at the start changes it from past tense (he punished) to present/future tense (he will punish)

steps.m: and he will punish severely (connected to يَجْزِىَ - reward)
steps.n: Added و to connect with the previous word يَجْزِىَ (reward), showing these actions happen together

steps.m: to spend or give away something

steps.n: The added alif shows someone is pretending or faking the action
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'someone who' and changed the word pattern to show the person doing the hypocritical action

steps.m: people who act two-faced (being punished by يُعَذِّبَ)
steps.n: Added ين to show many people. This word is receiving the punishment in the verse.
Added ال to point to specific hypocrites mentioned in the verse
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it introduces the condition 'if He wills' before the possible outcome of Allah turning in mercy to them (يَتُوبَ).
Added kasra mark under the alif to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past tense
Changed to a hollow verb form by adding a long alif between first and second root letters
This is a basic conjunction particle that connects two possibilities: either Allah will punish them or He will accept their repentance. It has no root since it's a particle.
Added the vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و
Changed from root letters to a verb by adding vowels

steps.m: he returns back (to what's right) (connected to إِن شَآءَ - if He wills)
steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show this is happening now or in the future. This word is connected to 'if He wills' in the ayah, showing Allah's mercy in accepting repentance.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of basic preposition
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attaching pronoun
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them'
Basic emphatic particle that strengthens statements. It makes what comes after it more definite and certain.
Added kasra (إِ) and shadda (نَّ) to show this is the strong form that affects the next word's ending. The shadda makes the emphasis stronger.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added a fatha mark to show this word is in the accusative case
Adding ال makes this the specific name Allah rather than just any deity
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'

steps.m: he was/existed (describing Allah's attributes غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to forgive or cover up mistakes

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who forgives a lot or repeatedly
Added tanween alif to show this describes the word كَانَ (was) in the ayah
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who is very merciful. In this ayah, it's describing Allah.
Added shadda on first letter because it comes after a word ending in a similar letter

steps.m: very merciful (describing Allah as a characteristic)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is describing Allah's characteristic in the sentence إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (Indeed Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful)
Added vowels (a-a-a) to the root letters to make it mean 'he returned'
Middle letter doubled with shadda to show stronger meaning
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word to what comes before it
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and changing the first letter. The ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in this sentence.
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) in the ayah, describing who did the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense singular form
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action

steps.m: to be filled with intense anger/rage

steps.n: This is the noun form showing the intense emotional state of rage. The word will be the object of the preposition بِ (with) in this ayah.
Added هِمْ (their) to show this rage belongs to them. This refers back to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: with their rage (describing how Allah sent them back)
steps.n: Added بِ (with) to show how Allah sent back those who disbelieved - they were sent back along with/accompanied by their rage, meaning they left angry and frustrated.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَنَالُوا۟) to show that something did not happen in the past. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added يَ at beginning to show present/future tense. This verb is negated by لَمْ which comes before it in the ayah, making it mean 'they did not obtain'
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'good thing'

steps.m: any good thing (object of يَنَالُوا - they did not obtain)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object (what wasn't obtained) in the sentence. The verb يَنَالُوا means 'they obtained/achieved', and with لَمْ before it, it means 'they did not obtain'.
Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past. The ي at the end changes to ى/ا because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: and He was sufficient (Allah being sufficient for the believers against their enemies)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement. In this ayah, Allah is saying He was enough/sufficient to protect the believers in battle.
This is the origin of the word Allah, related to worship and devotion
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to make it specific and unique, with special merger of letters for this holy name
Added emphasis with shadda and changed initial alif to wasl form
Added damma to show Allah is the subject doing the action of repelling in this verse
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed from a past action (trusted) to describe someone who believes/trusts as part of their character
Changed to show multiple believers
Added 'the' at beginning and case ending to show this word is being acted upon by the verb كَفَى (sufficed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he fought) to a noun (fighting/combat)

steps.m: the fighting (object of وَكَفَى - and [Allah] sufficed)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added fathas and the middle و changed to ا because it's a weak letter. This word tells us about Allah (اللَّهُ) in this ayah.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this description to what came before
Changed the first letter and added ال to make it specific to The One God

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of رَدَّ - repelled)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show Allah is doing the action of repelling
Root meaning 'strong' turned into an adjective meaning 'very strong' by adding shadda

steps.m: very strong (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added accusative case ending because this word describes Allah in the phrase 'وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ قَوِيًّا' (and Allah is very strong)
Root letters connected with vowels to make the adjective meaning 'strong'
Adding ي in the middle makes this show that Allah has complete and perfect might
Added ًا because this word comes after كَانَ (was) in the sentence وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ قَوِيًّا عَزِيزًا (and Allah was All-Strong, All-Mighty)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: to cause to descend, send down
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start makes someone cause the action - like changing 'went down' to 'made go down'

steps.m: and he sent down (referring to Allah sending down the people of the book from their fortresses)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start means 'and'. This connects this action to what happened before in the story.
This is the basic form used to point to someone or something specific
Changed to plural form to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the ones' instead of just 'ones'

steps.m: those who (connected to ظٰهَرُوهُم - supported them)
steps.n: Added final vowel marks and shadda for proper pronunciation. This word connects to ظٰهَرُوهُم (supported them) to tell us who did the supporting.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding long alif makes the meaning stronger and more intense
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they'
Added هُم to show who received the support
This is a primitive preposition (حرف جر) that shows origin, source or partitive meaning. It has no root as it's a particle, not a derived word.
Added shadda (doubled م) to show emphasis and connection with previous word
Added sukun at the end to show the word stops here. This word connects to أَهْلِ (people) to show 'from the people'.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a noun meaning the people who dwell or belong somewhere
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by الكتاب (the book), forming the phrase 'people of the book'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he wrote' (action) to 'book' (object) by changing middle vowel to long aa
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Changed to genitive case with kasra because it follows مِنْ أَهْلِ (from the people of). Also changed alif to alif with bar for proper Quranic spelling.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'the people' (أَهْلِ) to the verb 'supported them' (ظٰهَرُوهُم), showing who did the supporting.
The root forms the word for fortress or stronghold
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for 'fortress'
Changes the word from one fortress to many fortresses by adding ي and changing the ending
Adds هِمْ at the end to show these fortresses belong to them
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to create the past tense verb meaning 'he threw'

steps.m: and he threw (connected to أَنزَلَ/brought down)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous verb أَنزَلَ (brought down) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing location inside something. In this ayah, it shows that terror was put inside their hearts.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word in the Uthmani script of the Quran. This is just a writing style change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic singular word for 'heart'
This is a broken plural form, which means it changes the internal structure of the word rather than just adding a suffix

steps.m: their hearts (object of قَذَفَ فِى - cast into)
steps.n: The هِمُ suffix shows possession by a group. This word is the object of قَذَفَ فِى (cast into)
This is the basic concept of causing fear or terror
Changes from root meaning 'terrify' to the noun 'terror' by adding vowels (u,u,a)
The shadda makes the meaning stronger
ٱل makes it specific, like saying 'the' in English
Forms the word for a separate group or party
Changed from basic root 'separate' to show something that is already separated by adding vowel marks
Adding ي changes it from 'separated' to mean 'a group that is separated from others'

steps.m: a group (being acted upon by تَقْتُلُونَ وَتَأْسِرُونَ - you kill and take captive)
steps.n: Added an -an ending to show this group is the object (the ones being killed and captured)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense form
Changes from past tense (he killed) to present tense (kill) by changing the middle vowel from 'a' to 'u'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as the doer
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This verb acts on فَرِيقًا (a group) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: and you (all) capture (a group from أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتٰبِ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this with the previous action. This describes what is being done to some of the People of the Book mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to split or divide into parts

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a past tense verb by adding the vowels 'a' and 'i'
Added ي to show this is a distinct group that has been separated from others

steps.m: a group (being acted upon by تَقْتُلُونَ and تَأْسِرُونَ)
steps.n: Added an alif at the end to show this group is receiving the actions (being killed and captured)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes someone cause the inheritance to happen
كُمْ shows that the inheritance was given to multiple people ('you all')

steps.m: and he made you all inherit
steps.n: و connects this action to what came before it

steps.m: land (object of أَوْرَثَكُمْ - He made you inherit)
steps.n: Connected the root letters and added basic vowel marks. This word is what is being inherited in the phrase 'He made you inherit their land'.
Added fatha (small diagonal line) at the end to show this is the object being acted upon

steps.m: their land (what Allah made the believers inherit)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'their' to show this was the land belonging to the defeated people that Allah made the believers inherit
Changed the root letters to make a noun meaning 'place where someone lives' - like how 'house' comes from 'to live'
Changed the vowel to kasra and added ي to make it plural
Added هُم at the end to show the homes belong to them
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and', and added sukun at the end for proper pronunciation
Connected the root letters to form the basic singular noun for wealth
The word changed from single to plural by adding letters, like how 'money' becomes 'monies'

steps.m: their wealth (object of وَأَوْرَثَكُمْ - and He made you inherit)
steps.n: هُمْ shows the wealth belongs to them. This word is one of the objects of the verb وَأَوْرَثَكُمْ (and He made you inherit)

steps.m: and their wealth (object of وَأَوْرَثَكُمْ - and He made you inherit)
steps.n: Adds coordinating conjunction to connect to previous phrase
Connected the root letters and added basic vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added tanween to show this is the object being inherited. This word is what Allah made the believers inherit in the phrase 'He made you inherit their land'.
Added و to connect this with the previous items being inherited (their homes and their wealth)
Adds a mark to show this land is receiving the action
This is a primitive negative particle used with past tense. No morphological derivation since it's a particle.
Added emphasis to the ل with a shadda mark. This is a common strengthening of the negative particle when it comes after و or فَ.

steps.m: to tread upon, to step on

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all' by adding و
Added 'her/it' as the object being stepped on
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter

steps.m: and he was/existed (connected to أَوْرَثَكُمْ - He made you inherit)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous action in the verse
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال with special spelling (ٱل) and a shadda to show this is THE one true deity. The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence.
This is a preposition showing position or relationship. It's one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning - it shows the word ends with a long 'aa' sound.
Added damma and shadda to make it mean 'every/all'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the next word شَىْءٍ (thing), making it mean 'of every'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'thing'. This word is possessed by كُلِّ (every/all) in this ayah.
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word is possessed by كُلِّ (every/all). Also changed ي to ى which is just a spelling variation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changes from a past action (he had power) to a describing word meaning someone who has complete and constant power
The tanween alif shows this is describing Allah's attribute after the verb كَانَ (was)

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: The root letters combine with a doubled middle letter (shadda) to make a question word meaning 'which/who'
Added the calling particle يا with elongation (يٰٓ) to show someone is being addressed, and added damma to show it's the subject
Added ها to make it a complete calling phrase
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a noun
Added ي to show someone who does the action, like changing 'news' to 'news-bringer'
Adding ّ makes the word stronger and changes it from 'news-bringer' to 'prophet'
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific prophet
Added ُ to show this is the one being addressed and told to speak
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Making the last letter silent. This is telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to his wives, as shown by لِّأَزْوٰجِكَ (to your wives) coming right after.

steps.m: to join or unite as a pair

steps.n: Changed the word into a plural form by adding أ at the start and ا after و. This makes it mean multiple spouses instead of just one.
Added لِ (li) at start to mean 'to/for' + ال (al) merged into it making لِّ with shadda

steps.m: to your wives (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show these are 'your' wives. In this ayah, Allah is telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say this to his wives.
This is a conditional particle that shows what follows depends on something else. Here it introduces the condition 'if you desire' (تُرِدْنَ) which leads to the result 'then come' (فَتَعَالَيْنَ).
Changed the root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding fathas
Changed the first vowel to damma to begin making it a command form
Added تُ at the end to make it mean 'I was'
Changed from 'I' to 'you (women)' by adding نَّ at the end to show we're talking to multiple women
The middle letter و is removed to make a shorter command form of 'want'
Adding تُ changes it from 'want' to 'being wanted'
Added نَ at the end to make it 'you' for multiple women (like saying 'you all' for women only)
Changed from basic root 'to live' into a noun meaning 'living' by adding vowels (َ) and و
Changed from 'living' to 'life' by adding ة at the end - like changing 'alive' to 'life' in English

steps.m: the life (object of تُرِدْنَ - you want)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to mean 'the' and َ at the end because it's the object of the verb 'want' in this ayah
The root د-ن-و meaning 'near' was turned into دُنْيا which means 'worldly' or 'this life'. It's like saying 'the closer life' compared to the afterlife.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال
Changed from action to thing - like changing 'beautify' to 'beauty'
Added 'her' to show who owns the beauty. This is what is being wanted in the phrase 'you want worldly life and its beauty'
Added 'and' to connect this with 'worldly life' that comes before it

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at start and ا after first letter to change from 'he rose up' to a command meaning 'come here/come up'
Added ي at end to make it a command for one female
Added فَ at start to show this happens after something else
Added نَ at end to show this is speaking to multiple women
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger
Changed to passive - someone receives the enjoyment

steps.m: I will provide much enjoyment to you (feminine plural)
steps.n: Added كُنَّ at the end to show this is being done to multiple women (the Prophet's wives in this case)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter adds intensity to the meaning - like releasing many times or with force
Added أُ at beginning to mean 'I will' and changed the vowels to show future tense
Added كُنَّ at end to show the action is done to multiple women

steps.m: and I will release you (feminine plural)
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before
Changed to a noun meaning the act of releasing. This word emphasizes the action of the verb أُسَرِّحْكُنَّ (I will release you) in this ayah.
Added ًا (an) at the end to make it 'a release' - this shows it's being used as a description of how something is done. Like saying 'I will release you with a proper release'
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective describing beauty
Added ًا to show this word describes how the departure (سَرَاحًا) happens - it's a beautiful/graceful departure
This is a basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to كُنتُنَّ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what comes before it. This connects to the word كُنتُنَّ (you were) that follows it.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The middle weak letter و drops out and vowels change in past tense conjugation
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past (not 'you')
Added نَّ to show this refers to multiple female addressees
The و changes to ي in the present tense form
تُ at start makes it 'you' (feminine) in present tense
The ي is dropped to make a shorter form of 'want', and a sukun is added to keep it easy to say
The نَ ending shows this is addressing multiple females
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, the shadda emphasizes it, and the fatha at the end shows it's being acted upon in this sentence (by تُرِدْنَ - you all want/seek).
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot

steps.m: His messenger (object of تُرِدْنَ - you women want)
steps.n: Added هُ to show it belongs to Him (Allah), and َ to show this is being acted upon by the verb تُرِدْنَ (you women want)
Added وَ (and) to connect this word to اللَّهَ (Allah) before it, showing you want both Allah and His messenger
The root letters combine with the weak letter و becoming ا to form the noun for a dwelling place
Adding the fatha vowel mark shows this is a complete word, not just letters

steps.m: the home (object of تُرِدْنَ - you seek)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it definite ('the') and َ to show it's the object being sought

steps.m: and the home (object of تُرِدْنَ - you seek)
steps.n: Added و to connect with previous phrase about seeking Allah and His messenger
Added alif and changed vowels to make it mean 'the one who/that is last'
Adding ة makes it feminine and turns it into a special noun meaning 'the final life' or 'afterlife'

steps.m: the hereafter (object of تُرِدْنَ - you [women] desire)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the' hereafter) and َ to show it's what the women desire in this verse
This is an emphasis particle that makes the following noun accusative and adds certainty to the statement
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of the condition mentioned before (if you seek Allah and His messenger). The فَ connects this part to the verb تُرِدْنَ (you seek)
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'

steps.m: Allah (as the object of تُرِدْنَ - you all seek)
steps.n: The word 'god' (إِلَه) becomes the proper name 'Allah' (ٱللَّه) with special spelling. The -a ending shows Allah is being acted upon.
Added a-vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he prepared' in the past
Combined the doubled letters with a shadda
Added أَ at the beginning to show this is something that was prepared in the past and is complete

steps.m: to do good, to be excellent

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make it mean 'he became good/beautiful'
Added مُ at the start to make it mean 'the person who' and changed the middle vowels to make it a doer of the action
Added ات at the end to change from 'one who does good' to 'multiple women who do good'
Added لِ (for/to) and الْ (the) at the beginning, and ِ at the end because it follows the preposition لِ
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'among'
Added the feminine plural pronoun 'you' to show who is being addressed
Added emphasis to the pronoun with shadda

steps.m: to pay, reward, or give wages

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning the reward itself
The ًا ending shows this is the object being prepared by Allah
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing something becoming great
Changed the verb into an adjective by adding ي between second and third letters
Added tanween alif to show this is describing the reward (أَجْرًا) mentioned in the ayah
Root letters n-s-w became the common plural word for women by adding vowels
Added vocative particle يٰ to call out to the women
Added fatha mark at the end to show the accusative case
Changes from root meaning 'news' to mean 'someone who brings news' by adding vowels in the فَعِيل pattern
The doubled letter at the end makes the word stronger and more special
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ن gets doubled (شدّة) after ال.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show possession - meaning 'of the prophet' instead of just 'the prophet'
This is a conditional word that means 'whoever' and starts a condition. In this ayah, it refers to any of the Prophet's wives (mentioned before as يٰنِسَآءَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ) who might do something.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Basic meaning showing the concept of coming or arriving
Added يَ at beginning to show 'he' is doing the action in present/future time. Added ْ (sukun) on hamza to make pronunciation easier.

steps.m: he comes/arrives (in a conditional phrase with مَن)
steps.n: Removed final ي because this is part of a conditional sentence starting with مَن (whoever). In Arabic, verbs in conditional sentences take a special shortened form.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added كُن which shows this is addressing multiple women
Added shadda and fatha to emphasize the feminine plural ending

steps.m: to be shamefully bad or evil

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha and kasra) to make it mean 'he did something shameful' in the past
Changes the verb into a noun describing one instance of the bad action
Adds بِ at the beginning to show this is something being done with/by
Adds tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (any shamefully bad deed) and follows the preposition بِ. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style.

steps.m: to make very clear, to explain in detail
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger - like explaining something really well
Added مُ at start to show who does the action, and changed vowel pattern
Added ة at the end to make it feminine and change from 'one who makes clear' to 'something that is clear'
Added tanween kasra to show this describes فٰحِشَةٍ (shameful deed) from the ayah
The doubled م adds emphasis to show something is very clear/obvious
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding alif shows this action involves someone else - like doubling something for another person
Changed from 'he doubled' to 'is being doubled' by adding يُ at start and changing the vowels to show something is receiving the action

steps.m: it is doubled (passive verb in الْعَذَابُ)
steps.n: The word changes to show it's connected to 'the punishment' (العذاب). The line over the letter (ٰ) is just a different way of writing the same sound. The ending changes to be quiet (sukun).
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam

steps.m: for her/it (feminine) (referring to العَذَابُ - the punishment)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this is 'for her/it'. In this ayah, it refers to the punishment (العَذَابُ) mentioned right after this word.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added alif after second root letter to make it a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is the subject of يُضٰعَفْ (is multiplied).
Added ال at beginning to make it specific
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action. Also changed اَلْ to ٱلْ which is how it's usually written.
Changes the action of doubling into a noun meaning 'a double amount'
Adds the dual ending to show 'two of something'

steps.m: to be or exist in a state

steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas

steps.m: and he was/existed (connected to يُضٰعَفْ - it will be doubled)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement about punishment being doubled
Basic demonstrative pronoun that points to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Adding ل with kasra (ِ) makes it point to something even farther away
Adding ك with fatha (َ) completes the word, making it point to something mentioned before
Form used for feminine superlative (meaning 'highest')
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This word connects يَسِيرًا (easy) to اللَّهِ (Allah), showing that something is easy for Allah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال (al) and combining letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Root letters connected with vowels to make the basic adjective meaning 'easy'
Added ي to intensify the meaning of being easy

steps.m: easy (describing how something is for Allah)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how something is. This word tells us that 'that was easy for Allah' in this ayah.
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + من (whoever/who)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form

steps.m: he is devoutly obedient (subject of نُّؤْتِهَآ - We will give her)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows the ongoing nature of being obedient. In this ayah, this describes the condition for receiving the reward mentioned in نُّؤْتِهَآ (We will give her)
Basic preposition indicating source or selection from a group

steps.m: among you (feminine plural) (connected to يَقْنُتْ - who is devoutly obedient)
steps.n: Added feminine plural pronoun كُنَّ to show this refers to multiple women being addressed. This word connects back to يَقْنُتْ (who is devoutly obedient) in the ayah.
This is the base word - Allah's proper name. It's unique and doesn't come from a root.
Adding the kasra (ِ) at the end shows that something belongs to Allah or is connected to Him

steps.m: for/to Allah (connected to يَقْنُتْ - is devoutly obedient)
steps.n: Adding لِ at the beginning shows this is being done for Allah's sake. This connects to the verb يَقْنُتْ (is devoutly obedient) showing who the obedience is for.

steps.m: to send or dispatch (a message or messenger)

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'someone who sends messages' (like a job title)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with the previous phrase about Allah (اللَّهِ)
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something
Added هِ to show this messenger belongs to Allah, connecting back to اللَّهِ mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' (masculine) or 'she' does something now, not in the past

steps.m: and she works/does (connected to يَقْنُتْ - obeys)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous verb يَقْنُتْ (obeys)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed middle vowel to kasra to indicate a quality or state
Changed from 'was righteous' to 'righteous deed' by adding an alif and tanwin, making it a noun that receives the action of تَعْمَلْ (does/performs)
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added shadda on ن to emphasize the action
Added ها at the end to show who receives the action (her)

steps.m: we give her (in this ayah: We will give her reward twice)
steps.n: Extended the alif at the end for emphasis and proper Quranic writing style

steps.m: to pay back, give what is due
reward, compensation (object of نُّؤْتِ - we give)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning the reward itself. This word is what is being given (object) in the phrase 'we give her reward'.

steps.m: her reward (object of نُّؤْتِ - we give)
steps.n: Added ها to show the reward belongs to her or is for her. This whole phrase means 'we will give her her reward'.
The root letters were combined with a shadda (double letter) to make the past tense verb 'passed'
Added ة to make it mean 'one instance'
Added تَيْنِ to show exactly two times. This word describes how her reward will be doubled.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at start to show more direct or intensive preparation
Added sukun at end to prepare for adding suffix
Added و to connect this word with previous phrase
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added ها at the end to show this is 'for her'. The 'her' refers back to the reward (أَجْر) mentioned in this verse.

steps.m: to provide with what is needed to live

steps.n: Changed the action of providing into a noun meaning the actual thing that is provided, like food or resources

steps.m: a noble provision (object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show this is the object (thing receiving the action) in 'We have prepared a noble provision'
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowel marks to create an adjective showing a permanent quality
Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this describes how something is done. Here it describes how Allah has prepared the provision - in a noble/generous way.
Changed the root into the plural form for 'women' by adding long alif and hamza
Added يٰ at the beginning to show these women are being called or addressed directly. Also added fatha at the end and lengthened the alif to show emphasis in the calling.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic noun
Changed the middle letter and added ي to make it mean 'person who brings news'
Added ال (the) at the start to mean 'the prophet' instead of just 'a prophet'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by or connected to something else in the sentence
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This verb is special because even though it's in past tense form, it means present tense.
Changed from 'he is not' to 'you are not' by adding تَ at the end. The ي gets dropped because it's a weak letter.
Added نَّ to show we're talking to multiple women with emphasis. This matches the context where the verse is addressing the Prophet's wives.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic word meaning 'one'
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word is indefinite and affected by the preposition كَ that comes before it

steps.m: like anyone (addressing wives of Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added كَ (meaning 'like/similar to') at the beginning. In this ayah, it's telling the Prophet's wives that they aren't like any other women due to their special status.
The shadda emphasizes the م and the fatha is added as a connecting vowel
This is an irregular plural form specifically for 'women'
Changes from the root meaning 'woman' into the irregular plural 'women' - one of the special plural forms in Arabic
Adds the definite article to make the noun definite
Adds kasra at the end because this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects 'if you are mindful of Allah' with what follows.
The sukun (ْ) changes to kasra (ِ) because it comes before a word starting with a hamzat wasl (connecting hamza). This is just for easier pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning which changes the meaning to doing the action to oneself. The و changed to ت because it's easier to pronounce.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added نَّ to change from 'I' to 'you women (plural)'
Changed the spelling of the beginning اِ to ٱ, but the pronunciation stays the same
This is the basic negation word used to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic

steps.m: so/then not (connects to تَخْضَعْنَ - be soft)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَخْضَعْنَ (be soft), telling the wives of the Prophet what not to do as a result of their special status.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at start changes 'he' to 'you'

steps.m: you (feminine plural) submit (addressing the Prophet's wives)
steps.n: Added نَ at the end to show this is addressing multiple women. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing the Prophet's wives, telling them not to speak in a soft or overly submissive way.
This pattern turns the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of doing something'
Changed from action (to say) into a thing (speech/saying)
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the speech' instead of just any speech
The ِ shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تَخْضَعْنَ (be soft/submissive)
The بِ shows how the action happens - they shouldn't be soft/submissive with their speech
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and added ُ at end to show it's happening now

steps.m: and then he desires/covets (in the hearts of those who have disease)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to mean 'and then/so', and changed the ending to َ because it follows فَ. This verb describes what might happen in the hearts of those with disease if the Prophet's wives speak too softly.
Composed of definite article ال merged with relative pronoun ذي, with shadda on ل
Basic preposition showing location or position inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it's used to show location inside someone's heart, describing where the sickness/disease is.
The root meaning becomes 'heart' because the heart is seen as constantly changing and turning emotions
Adding ِ shows this heart belongs to someone or follows a word like 'in' or 'from'

steps.m: his heart (possessed by the one who)
steps.n: Adding هِۦ means 'his' - it shows the heart belongs to 'the one who' (الَّذِى) that was mentioned before. This whole phrase comes after the word 'in' (فِى), telling us where something is located.
Changed from basic root meaning 'to be sick' into the noun 'sickness' by adding the vowels a-a
Added tanween (double damma) to make it mean 'a sickness' instead of just 'sickness'
The middle و is dropped and ُ is added on first letter to show past tense. The نَ shows multiple women did this action.

steps.m: and they (women) said (connected to تَخْضَعْنَ - be soft in speech)
steps.n: Adds وَ at beginning to connect this action with the previous command not to be soft in speech
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'speech'. This word is what is being said in the command قُلْنَ (say [you women]!).
Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show this is 'a speech' - the thing being said. Like adding 'a/an' in English.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to passive participle form showing something that is known or recognized, thus acceptable
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added tanween alif to show this describes how they should speak (referring back to قَوْلًا - a statement)

steps.m: to settle or stay in one place

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'settled' to 'stay!' by making it a command for women - the middle letter gets quiet (sukun) and we add ن at the end
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is a basic preposition showing location. It connects with the word بُيُوتِكُنَّ (your houses) to show where the staying/remaining should happen.
The ي at the end changes to ى when written at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun for a single house
Changed the middle vowel from 'ay' to 'u' and added 'uu' to make it plural (broken plural pattern)
Added kasra because it comes after the preposition في
Added كُنَّ to show the houses belong to multiple female addressees
This is a negative particle that means 'not' or 'do not'. In this ayah, it's used with a command to tell the Prophet's wives what not to do.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this prohibition with the previous command in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding تَ at the beginning makes the action reflexive - done to oneself
Adding نَ at the end makes it feminine plural past tense

steps.m: to display prominently or make apparent

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Adding تَ at the beginning shows doing the action to oneself - in this context, it refers to women displaying themselves in an improper way
Changed vowel pattern to make it a verbal noun (the act of displaying oneself)
This form describes someone who has this quality of lacking knowledge
The يَّة ending changes this from describing a person to describing a whole way of life or time period

steps.m: of the state of ignorance (possessed by تَبَرُّجَ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it's owned by the word تَبَرُّجَ (display/behavior), making the full phrase mean 'the display of ignorance'
Added damma on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a basic noun
Added alif maqsura at the end to make it feminine and indicate it's a superlative (meaning 'most first' or 'foremost')

steps.m: the first (feminine) (describing الجٰهِلِيَّةِ - period of ignorance)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال to specify this particular period. The word is describing الجٰهِلِيَّةِ (the period of ignorance) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he stood' to 'you stand!' (command). The middle letter و gets dropped to make it easier to say.
Added نَ to make it a command for multiple female addressees
Added و meaning 'and' to connect this command with previous commands in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it a noun for one instance of prayer
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the ص with shadda

steps.m: the ritual prayer (object of أَقِمْنَ - establish)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show it's the object of the verb أَقِمْنَ (establish), and changed spelling with long alif (ٰ) and و for writing style
Adding آ at the start makes it mean 'cause to come' which becomes 'give'
Adding kasra at end makes it a command for a female
Adding نَ makes this a command for multiple females
Adding وَ connects this command to the previous commands in the verse

steps.m: to purify or make something grow better

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Adding ة changes the verb into a noun for the act itself
Adding ا makes it a stronger noun form emphasizing the act of purification
Adding ال (the) makes it refer to the specific religious duty of giving charity
The ا is written as وٰ and fatha is added at the end because this word is receiving the action of giving (وَءَاتِينَ meaning 'and give')
Forms the basic command by adding hamza at start and kasra in middle. The weak letter و drops out in command form.
Adds نَ to make the command for multiple females
Adds و at the beginning to connect this command with the previous commands in the verse
Changed to the unique name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in وَأَطِعْنَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being obeyed in this verse. The word connects to the verb وَأَطِعْنَ (and obey) where Allah is the one being obeyed.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added fatha to show this is the object of the verb 'obey' in the command 'obey Allah and His messenger'
Added هُ to show this is His (Allah's) messenger
Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous word (Allah) in 'obey Allah and His messenger'
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'verily' in English
Adding ما changes the meaning from just emphasis to restriction - showing that something is the only case. Here it emphasizes that Allah only wants this specific thing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
The middle و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Changed from past tense (رَادَ) to present tense (يُرِيدُ) by adding يُ at the start. The word shows what Allah wants or wills in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing something to happen
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: so that he removes (something from أَهْلَ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose/intent. In this ayah, it shows Allah's intention to remove something from the People of the House (أَهْلَ)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'from'. It's one of the common Arabic prepositions that shows separation or distance.
Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُم to show who is being addressed. This refers to multiple people.
Added the final damma (ُ) to show this word is the object of the verb يُذْهِبَ (to remove). Allah wants to remove something from you.
Root letters were given vowels ِ and ْ to make a noun meaning 'filth' from the basic idea of being filthy
Added ال to point to a specific impurity. The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it's a 'sun letter' that combines with the ل sound

steps.m: the impurity (object of يُذْهِبَ - to remove)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this is what's being removed (object of يُذْهِبَ meaning 'to remove')

steps.m: to settle, dwell, or belong somewhere

steps.n: Changes from root letters to a noun meaning 'family'
Added a fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon and belongs to البيت (the house) that comes after it.
Changes from verb 'to dwell' into noun 'house' by adding specific vowel marks
Added ال to make it specific ('the house' instead of just 'house')
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word أَهْل (family/people) in a possession relationship
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The doubled middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Changed to present tense with يُ at start and kasra under middle letter
Added 'and' to connect this action with previous actions in the verse

steps.m: and he thoroughly purifies you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being purified (you all)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb 'made pure' into a noun meaning 'thorough cleaning/purifying' by adding تَ and ي
The word gets an -an ending (تَطْهِيرًا) to show it's the object - the thing that Allah wants to do
Changed to command form by adding vowel marks
Adds 'and' to connect with previous statement
Changes command to be for multiple women
Added وَ at start to mean 'and', and the alif changed to hamza-wasl (ٱ)

steps.m: that which (connects to يُتْلَىٰ - is recited)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb after it. It's like saying 'that which' in English, as in 'remember that which is recited.'
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb pattern
Adding يُ at the beginning shows this is happening now/present, and the vowel changes show it's being done to something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: it is being recited (what is being recited: مَا)
steps.n: The و changes to ى with alif above it because it's at the end of the word. This is a common spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: in (connects بُيُوتِكُنَّ with what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'your houses' (بُيُوتِكُنَّ) with what should be remembered.
Changes root letters into a simple noun meaning 'house' by adding vowel marks
Changes singular to plural by changing vowel pattern and adding و
Adds kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Adds suffix to show the houses belong to multiple female addressees

steps.m: from (connects ءَايٰتِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing source, origin, or starting point. Here it connects 'verses' (ءَايٰتِ) with what should be remembered from the previous part.

steps.m: to show a sign or mark
steps.n: Connected the root letters together
Changed from a verb meaning 'to show signs' into a noun meaning 'one sign/verse'
Changed from singular to plural by adding ات
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ِ shows it belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.
Added vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Adding ة turns the general idea of wisdom into a specific instance or piece of wisdom
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
Adding the conjunction prefix to connect with previous phrase
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more definite and certain. It also causes the next word (ٱللَّهَ) to take a fatha at the end.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the doubled ل sound.
Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed) in this ayah. Also changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added basic past tense vowel pattern to show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a hollow verb

steps.m: gentle, kind, subtle (as a constant quality)

steps.m: to join as a pair, to marry
steps.n: This root is about joining things in pairs, especially marriage

steps.m: to cause to marry, to give in marriage
steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes it mean 'to cause/make someone marry' instead of just 'to marry'
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action
Adding ك shows you (masculine singular) are receiving the action
Adding ها shows she/her is the object being married to you. In this ayah, this refers to Zaynab (رضي الله عنها) who was married to Zayd (رضي الله عنه) before.
This is a basic particle that shows purpose or intention

steps.m: so that, in order to (connects to يَكُونَ)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to emphasize the purpose. This compound particle introduces the reason why Allah made the Prophet marry Zaynab - so that there would be no difficulty for believers regarding marriages to ex-wives of adopted sons.
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يَكُونَ (to be) into its opposite meaning. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
This is the basic root meaning to exist or be
Changes the root letters into a present tense verb by adding damma marks
Adds يَ at the beginning to show present tense and who is doing the action
Adds fatha at end to show this is part of a longer sentence

steps.m: upon (connected to يَكُونَ - there would be)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or against something else. In this ayah, it's used with يَكُونَ to show what would be placed upon the believers.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from a verb (to believe) into a noun meaning 'one who believes'. The أ from the root word changes to ؤ when making this type of noun.
The suffix ين is added to create the regular masculine plural form, indicating multiple believers. This is one of the most common plural formations in Arabic.
Added ال at the beginning to mean 'the' and َ at the end because this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Adding tanween damma makes this a noun meaning 'a difficulty'
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that something is being hidden inside yourself (نَفْسِكَ).
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the root into a noun meaning one who is paired with another
This is a common broken plural pattern for words describing pairs or types

steps.m: of the spouses (belonging to their adopted sons)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word belongs to أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ (their adopted sons) in the verse
Changes the root 'to call/claim' into a noun meaning 'someone who is claimed by others (adopted)'
Changes from singular to plural by adding أ at start and اء at end - like going from 'adopted child' to 'adopted children'
Changes from regular plural to a possessed plural (meaning 'your adopted ones')
Adds pronoun to show who possesses the adopted ones
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a specific time in the past

steps.m: when, whenever (in the future) (introduces condition for قَضَوْا)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes the time reference from past to future/conditional. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'when they have accomplished' (قَضَوْا)
Added fathas to make it past tense. The ي at the end becomes ى because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: they completed/fulfilled (referring to believers in أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This refers back to believers mentioned earlier.

steps.m: they completed/fulfilled (referring to believers in أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added a small circle (۟) above to mark the plural. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added the basic masculine singular pronoun هُ (him/it)
Changed the singular masculine pronoun to plural feminine by adding نَّ
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form a noun
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being fulfilled in the phrase 'when Zayd had fulfilled his need of her'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of a past tense verb

steps.m: and he was (connected to قَضَىٰ - completed)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous action in the verse - when Zayd completed his purpose with her, and (then) he was...
Changed from action to noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the thing itself rather than doing it.
Added ُ at the end to show this is the first word in the phrase 'command of Allah' (أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ)
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on ل, and changed initial alif. This word is possessed by أَمْرُ (command) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from active (doing) to passive (being done to) by adding مَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and adding ول at end
Added ًا at the end to show this word completes the meaning of كَانَ (was)
Starting with the basic question word مَ and adding ا makes it more general, like changing 'what' to 'whatever'
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on م changes this from a question word to a negative word. Here it's used with كَانَ to say something 'was not'
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.

steps.m: he was (describes a past state in this ayah)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
This is the basic form of the word that means something being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of or affecting another. Here it shows something affecting the Prophet.
Added vowels and ي to make it mean 'someone who brings news from Allah'
The doubled ي (shown by ّ) makes the word stronger, showing someone who regularly brings divine news
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, and the ن gets a shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by the word عَلَى (upon) that comes before it
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects the word حَرَجٍ (difficulty/blame) with what follows, showing there is no blame coming from that source.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning the result of being constrained
Added the two dots below (ٍ) to show this is 'any/a' constraint, connected to 'from/of the Prophet'
Added kasra vowel mark to show the word's pronunciation

steps.m: in what (that which فَرَضَ Allah ordained)
steps.n: Added ما which makes this word ask about or refer to something. In this ayah, it refers to what Allah ordained.

steps.m: to make something required or necessary

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what Allah did in relation to أَمْرُ (command/matter) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for him (referring to النَّبِىِّ - the Prophet)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the action is for. This refers back to the Prophet mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper showing that the هُ should be pronounced with a long 'u' sound

steps.m: to establish a path or way

steps.n: Added damma and doubled the middle letter to show this is a strong, established concept

steps.m: a specific established way or practice
steps.n: Added ة to show this is one specific instance of the concept
Added fatha to show this is being acted upon or connected to Allah in this ayah
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word owns/possesses 'command' (أَمْرُ) in this ayah. Also changed alif to wasl alif since it comes after another word.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects what Allah prescribed with those who came before.
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things

steps.m: those who (plural, referring to people in the past)
steps.n: Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to make it specific and definite, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'ones who'
Added vowels to connect the letters and form basic past tense. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: they passed away (referring to those who came before)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. The ا changes back to و because it's followed by وا.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's used with حَرَجٍ (difficulty) to show there is no difficulty starting from or coming upon the Prophet.
This form turns the concept of 'before' into a word that tells when something happened
Added damma ending to show this is a complete adverb of time, meaning 'before' in the context of 'those who passed away before'
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks. The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb in past tense. This word tells about أَمْرُ (command) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun
Added fatha mark to make it a proper noun
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was) in the phrase وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ (and Allah's command was...)
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word describes whose way/practice (سُنَّةَ) it is. The alif at the start becomes a wasl form (ٱ).
Added fathas to connect the root letters and form a noun meaning 'decree' or 'destiny'
Added an -an ending to show this is receiving the action (like saying 'a destiny' instead of just 'destiny')
Changed to passive participle meaning 'predetermined'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show this is something that was determined, not someone doing the determining
Added shadda on م to emphasize that this is absolutely determined
Added tanween to show this describes Allah's decree mentioned earlier in the ayah (أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ)
Added ال to make it definite and specific. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis on the ذ.

steps.m: those who (subject of يُبَلِّغُونَ - they convey)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to يُبَلِّغُونَ (they convey) in the ayah, describing the people who are doing the conveying.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with extra effort or repeatedly
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to رِسٰلٰتِ ٱللَّهِ (the messages of Allah) which comes after it.
Changed from basic root meaning 'send' into a noun meaning 'message/delivery' by adding vowels and ا
Added ة at the end to make it mean one specific instance of something being sent - a message
Changed to plural form with ٰتِ ending. This word is what's being delivered in the phrase 'those who deliver messages'
Changed to the unique name for the One God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show this describes whose messages they are delivering. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The final يَ changes to ى because it's at the end of a present tense verb.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The و before ن has a sukun (ْ) to show it's part of the plural marker, not a long vowel.
Added هُۥ (him) to show who they fear. This هُۥ is the object (receiver) of the fear.
Added the conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this clause with what precedes it
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَخْشَوْنَ (they fear), showing they do not fear
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement وَيَخْشَوْنَهُۥ (and they fear Him)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together. This shows something that happened in the past.
The ي at the start shows this is happening now or in the future. The final ي changes to ى because it comes at the end.

steps.m: they are afraid (referring to those who deliver Allah's messages)
steps.n: Adding ون changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This refers back to 'those who deliver' (ٱلَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ) from earlier in the verse.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a basic noun meaning one person
Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being feared in the phrase 'they do not fear anyone'

This is a basic particle used to show an exception - it connects what comes before it (أَحَدًا meaning 'anyone') to what comes after it (ٱللَّهَ meaning 'Allah'), showing that Allah is the exception to 'not fearing anyone'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence
Added fathas to make it past tense. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: and he was sufficient (connected to يَخْشَوْنَ - they fear)
steps.n: Added 'and' (و) at the start to connect to the previous phrase about fearing Allah. The alif at the end is just part of how this word is written.
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is treated as a proper noun.
The shadda emphasizes the 'L' sound, which is a distinctive feature in the name Allah
The بِ at the beginning shows this is being used to express doing something with or by Allah's help. The ِ at the end shows this connects to other words in the sentence.

steps.m: to count or keep track of something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to count'
Added ي to show this describes someone who does this action thoroughly or professionally

steps.m: as a thorough reckoner (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا (an) at the end to make it work as a description of Allah in this specific sentence
This is a negative particle that does not come from a root. It works with كَانَ to say something 'was not' true.
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م to show strong pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning
Added basic past tense vowels to show who did the action and when
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic for hollow verbs.

steps.m: to praise or commend something good

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and doubled the middle letter to show this describes someone who receives lots of praise
Added two dots above (tanween) to show this is Muhammad's name when he is doing something

steps.m: to nurture/care for as a father

steps.n: Changed to أَبَا because it's being used as 'father' when something happened to him (object form)
The alif is lengthened to emphasize the word's role as an object
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the letters to make the word for 'one'

Added tanwin kasra to show this word is indefinite and is being acted upon by مِن (from/of) in the phrase. Here it means 'anyone' in the context of 'Muhammad is not the father of anyone among your men.'
This is a preposition that shows origin, source or partitive meaning
Added shadda for emphasis and to connect smoothly with the previous word. This preposition connects to رِجَالِكُمْ (your men) to show Muhammad was not from among their men.
The vowels show this is a noun meaning a person/thing with this quality
Changes to broken plural form to show multiple men
Added kasra at end to show possession relationship
Added suffix كُمْ meaning 'your (plural)'
The dot above the ر gets a shadda mark because it comes after a word ending in ن
This is the basic contrasting particle that shows an exception or contrast to what was said before

steps.m: and yet/but still (contrasting with مَّا كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبَآ أَحَدٍ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this contrast with the previous statement. This shows that while Muhammad was not a father to any of the men, he was still something else (the Messenger of Allah)
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
The letter ر gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in ن or م
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by كَانَ (was) in the sentence
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific god
Adding kasra shows this word is being described by another word
Added fatha vowels and alif to show this is someone/something that seals or completes. This word is connected to النَّبِيِّۦنَ (the prophets) to show Muhammad is their final seal.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it in the verse.
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: prophet, one who brings divine news
steps.n: Changes from verb (bringing news) to noun (person who brings news)
Adds 'the' (ال) at start and changes the ending to make it specific
Changes from 'the prophet' to 'the prophets' by adding ين at the end, and shows it belongs to the word خَاتَمَ (seal of)
The root letters ك-و-ن became the past tense verb كَانَ (he was) by adding vowels and changing و to ا in the middle
Added و at beginning to connect this word with the previous phrase: 'Muhammad was not the father of any of your men, but [he was] the Messenger of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Adding 'al' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity
Adding damma shows this word is the subject of the sentence
The shadda doubles the middle letter to show completeness. The damma shows this is the base form.
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes before it (the preposition بِ). This whole phrase describes how Allah is عَلِيمًا (All-Knowing).
The preposition بِ (with/by) connects this word to عَلِيمًا (All-Knowing), showing that Allah is All-Knowing with/of every thing
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun. This word is possessed by كُلِّ (every/all) in this ayah.
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this noun is indefinite and is being possessed by another word
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'

steps.m: All-Knowing (as an eternal quality of Allah)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knew' (past action) to 'All-Knowing' (permanent quality) by adding ي to make it like an adjective
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'Allah was All-Knowing'

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: The root letters combine together and the middle letter gets doubled (with shadda) to make a word that asks 'which?'
Adding يٰ with alif to call attention, like saying 'hey' to get someone's attention
Adding the damma (u sound) shows we are calling out to someone directly
Adding ها makes it a complete calling phrase
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who' or 'which'. We add ي to make it work with other words.
Adding ين makes it talk about many people instead of just one person
Adding ال at the beginning makes it specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'ones who'
Final changes: 1) ال becomes ٱلّ with a shadda for stronger pronunciation, 2) add َ at the end to connect it with the next word ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'to make someone believe' instead of just 'to trust'
Added وا to show many people did this action. This word is connected to ٱذْكُرُوا۟ (remember/mention) in the ayah - the same group of believers is being told to remember Allah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Removed first vowel, changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command to one person
Added helper alif at start to make it easier to pronounce

steps.m: remember! (all of you) (command to believers in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Changed to plural command and modified alif to match Uthmani script. This command is directed at the believers mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added fatha mark to show this word is affected by something before it

steps.m: remembrance (the act or state of remembering)
steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'remember' into a noun meaning 'remembrance' by adding the vowel marks. Like changing 'remember' to 'memory'.

steps.m: remembrance (object of اذْكُرُوا/remember in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what is being remembered. In this ayah, it connects to the command اذْكُرُوا (remember) that comes before it, showing what should be remembered much.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
Changed the vowels and added ي to make it an adjective describing a quality
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the remembrance (ذِكْرًا) is done - abundantly
Doubling of middle root letter shows this is done with full effort and commitment

steps.m: all of you praise/glorify! (as a command)
steps.n: Changes to a command for multiple people to do this action together

steps.m: and all of you praise/glorify! (as a command)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect with previous statement

steps.m: and glorify Him! (as a command to a group)
