steps.n: Adds pronoun هُ meaning 'Him' to show Allah is the one being glorified. This is a command to multiple people.
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'early morning'
Adds feminine ending to specify one instance of early morning

steps.m: in the early morning (time of glorifying اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatha to show this is when the glorification (تَسْبِيح) should happen. This answers 'when' for the command وَسَبِّحُوهُ (and glorify Him)
The ي makes it an adjective describing something with this quality

steps.m: in the late afternoon (describing when سَبِّحُوهُ/glorify Him happens)
steps.n: Adds ًا to show this describes when the glorification happens. This is connected to the verb سَبِّحُوهُ (glorify Him) from earlier in the verse.

steps.m: and in the late afternoon (describing when سَبِّحُوهُ/glorify Him happens)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect with previous time

steps.m: He (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun that points to someone being talked about. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is mentioned as the one who sends blessings upon the believers.

steps.m: the one who (connects هُوَ to يُصَلِّى)
steps.n: This is a special connecting word that joins هُوَ (He) to its description يُصَلِّى (prays/sends blessings). It helps explain which 'He' we're talking about.

The root letters were connected with fatha marks and a shadda was added to the middle letter to show doing something a lot (like praying many times). The last letter changed to ى because that's how this type of word ends.
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense and changed middle vowel to kasra. This word refers back to هُوَ (He) in the ayah, showing that Allah is the one sending blessings.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun

steps.m: upon you all (object of يُصَلِّى - he sends blessings)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word is receiving the action of يُصَلِّى (sending blessings) in this ayah.

steps.m: to have power or control over something

steps.n: Changed the root 'to have power' into 'angel' by adding the vowel marks a-a

steps.m: angel (with emphasis on being a messenger)
steps.n: Added alif to make it a noun emphasizing the angel's role as a messenger
Changes the word to broken plural pattern for angels
Adds feminine marker ت to make it a feminine plural noun
Adds nominative case ending
Added هُ to show these are Allah's angels

steps.m: and His angels (connected to يُصَلِّى - sends blessings)
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect to previous word. The alif is written as ٰٓ in this Quranic spelling
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to make it present tense and causative - showing someone making someone else do the action

steps.m: so that he makes (someone) go out
steps.n: Adds purpose/intent to the verb

steps.m: so that he makes you all go out (from الظُّلُمٰتِ/darkness)
steps.n: Adds second person masculine plural object pronoun. This shows who is being made to go out - in this case, from darkness as mentioned in the following words.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the 'م' sound because it comes after the word 'ٱلَّذِى' in this ayah.
The fatha (َ) is added at the end because this preposition is followed by the definite noun ٱلظُّلُمٰتِ (the darknesses).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he did it) to a noun (the thing itself) by changing the vowels
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple types or instances of darkness
Added ٱل to make it specific and ِ because it comes after the word مِنَ (from). The shadda emphasizes the ظ sound.
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the word النُّورِ (the light) to show movement from darkness towards light.
Adding damma (ُ) changes this from a verb into a noun meaning 'light'
Adding sukoon (ْ) makes this a complete noun form
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word إِلَى (to/towards) in the ayah
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: and he was (connected to رَحِيمًا - merciful)
steps.n: Added و (and) at the beginning to connect this word to what comes after it, showing 'and He was'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to believe) to a noun meaning 'believer'. The م at the start and ؤ in the middle show it's about the person who does the believing.
Regular masculine plural form. The word مُؤْمِنِين is the object of كَانَ (was) in this ayah, showing who Allah was merciful to.
Added definite article ال to make the word definite

steps.m: to/with the believers (related to رَحِيمًا - merciful)
steps.n: Added بِ to show connection to رَحِيمًا (merciful), indicating Allah was merciful to/with the believers
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
This form shows someone who has this quality as a permanent characteristic (like changing 'show mercy' to 'merciful')
The ًا ending shows this word is describing how Allah was (كَانَ). This is a special form used in the Quran to emphasize Allah's perfect mercy.
Changed from basic 'live' root to stronger 'give life' meaning by adding vowels and doubling middle letter
Added تَ to make it 'you' form of the verb
Added ة at the end to change from 'to greet' (verb) into 'a greeting' (noun)
Added ُ to show this is the subject
Added هُمْ to show possession by them
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Adding vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The final vowel changes to ى to show it's happening now
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to و because of this addition

steps.m: they meet him (on the Day they meet Him)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who they are meeting. This word relates to يَوْمَ (day) before it, showing what happens on that day
Root letters س-ل-م were joined with short vowels (َ) to make the basic noun meaning peace
Added a long 'aa' sound to make the word stronger and more formal, like saying 'peeeace' instead of 'peace'
Added two dots above to make it mean 'a peace' instead of 'the peace'
Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make 'he prepared'
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: and he prepared (for them a reward)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this action with what came before in the verse
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose

steps.m: for them (receiving أَجْرًا كَرِيمًا - a noble reward)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is receiving the action. This connects to أَجْرًا كَرِيمًا (a noble reward) in the ayah, showing who will receive the reward.
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'reward' by adding the vowels a and u

steps.m: a reward (object of أَعَدَّ - He has prepared)
steps.n: Added an alif and tanween (double marks) at the end to show this is receiving the action - it's the reward that Allah prepared
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
The ي between second and third letters with kasra on first letter makes it describe someone/something with this quality
The ًا ending changes this from just the quality 'noble' to mean 'a noble thing' that is receiving the action
The root letters أ-ي-ي combine to form أَيّ with a shadda, making it mean 'which' or 'who' when asking questions
Adding يَ at the start turns this into a calling word, like saying 'O you who...'
Adding damma (ُ) at the end shows this word is in the nominative case
Adding هَا makes it clear you're calling someone's attention
ها makes it a formal way to call someone
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic noun form
Changes from 'news' to 'news-bringer' by adding special letters that make it mean a person with this special job
Makes it specific by adding 'the' and emphasizes the middle letter
Adds the ُ mark to show this is being directly addressed in 'O Prophet!'
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain
Added نا (we) at the end, written as آ in Quranic spelling. This word connects to the verb أَرْسَلْنٰكَ (we sent you) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start and changing vowels makes the verb causative - showing someone made the sending happen
Adding نَا shows 'we' did the action
Adding كَ shows who was sent - in this case referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. The alif in نَا changes to ٰ when connected to كَ.
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action, like changing 'witness' to 'witness-er'
Added tanwin (double fatha) to show this is any witness, not a specific one

steps.m: as a witness (describes the Prophet ﷺ in أَرْسَلْنٰكَ)
steps.n: The alif in شَاهِد became a long alif (ٰ), and tanween was added at the end to show it describes the Prophet ﷺ's role as a witness
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added مُ at beginning and shadda on ش to show someone who does this action repeatedly

steps.m: and one who gives good news (intensively)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and as a bearer of good news (describing النَّبِىُّ)
steps.n: Added an -an ending to show this is describing the Prophet (النَّبِىُّ) as 'a giver of good news'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

Changes the word from 'he warned' to 'one who warns' by adding ي between the last two root letters and changing the middle vowel to kasra (ِ). This pattern is used to show someone who does something as their role or characteristic.

steps.m: and a warner (connected to مُبَشِّرًا - bearer of good news)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this word with مُبَشِّرًا (bearer of good news) that comes before it in the ayah

steps.m: and as a warner (describing how Allah sent the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatah (ًا) to show this describes how Allah sent the Prophet ﷺ, connecting to أَرْسَلْنٰكَ (We sent you) earlier in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he called' in the past tense. The last letter و changes to ا in this form.
Changed from verb 'he called' to noun meaning 'one who calls' by adding alif after first letter
Added an alif and tanween (اً) at the end to show this is describing the Prophet ﷺ along with the word 'lamp' (سراجاً)

steps.m: and a caller/inviter (describing the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with other descriptions of the Prophet ﷺ
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'calling' (دَاعِيًا) with 'Allah' (ٱللَّهِ) to show who is being called to.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
The root letters أذن became the noun 'permission' by adding إِ at the start and making the middle letter silent
Added بِ at the start to show this is done 'by means of' permission
Added ِ at the end to show this connects to the next word
Added هِۦ at the end to show this is His permission. The ۦ is just a pronunciation helper.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he illuminated' changes to become a noun meaning 'lamp' - something that does the illuminating. Like changing 'write' to 'pen'.
The ًا ending makes it 'a lamp' (not 'the lamp') and connects it with 'a caller' mentioned earlier in the verse
The و connects this word to دَاعِيًا (caller) that came before it, adding another quality or role
The middle letter و changes to ي to match the vowel sounds around it, making it easier to say
Added مُ at the beginning to show this describes something that does the action
Adding shadda (doubling) on ي makes the meaning stronger - something that gives a lot of light
Added tanween fatHa to show this word describes سِرَاجًا (lamp) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: to give much good news, announce glad tidings
steps.n: Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to make the verb more intense and emphatic
Changed to a command form - telling someone to give good news (adding kasra at the end connects it smoothly to the next word المؤمنين)
Added و at the beginning to connect this command to what came before it

steps.m: to trust, to believe in something deeply

steps.n: Changed the root into someone who does the action by adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels. Like changing 'believe' to 'believer' in English.

steps.m: believers (the ones receiving good news from بَشِّر)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it mean multiple believers. This word is receiving the action of 'giving good news' from the word بَشِّر before it.

steps.m: the believers (the ones receiving good news from بَشِّر)
steps.n: Adding ال and َ makes it mean these specific believers, not just any believers. The َ shows this word is receiving the action.
This is a word that adds emphasis when telling about something new

steps.m: with [the fact] that (connects to وَبَشِّرِ - and give good news)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the start connects this word to وَبَشِّرِ (give good news) from earlier in the verse, showing what the good news is about
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to المؤمنين (the believers) to show something is for them.
Adding هم shows this refers to the believers (المؤمنين) mentioned earlier in the ayah. It shows something is for that group.
This shows where something comes from or what it's made of
The added fatha (َ) helps connect 'min' smoothly to the next word (Allah)
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed to the unique name of the one true God in Islam. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it.

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to create a noun meaning the result of giving extra. This word is described by كَبِيرًا (great) which comes after it.
Added tanween fatha (ًا) at the end to show this noun is indefinite and in accusative case
Added ي between second and third letters and vowels to make it into an adjective showing a strong quality
Added ًا at the end to show this describes the bounty (فَضْلًا) mentioned in the ayah
This is a negative particle used before verbs to tell someone not to do something
The و connects this command with previous instructions in the ayah
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense. The middle letter و becomes ي because it has a kasra (i-sound) before it - this is a common pattern in Arabic verbs
Added تُ at the beginning to show 'you' as the doer
Changed to command form with kasra ending. The ي is dropped in the command form. This word appears after لا meaning 'do not obey!'
Added vowels to make a complete past tense verb
Changes verb into noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Makes word plural and shows it's being acted upon by the verb تُطِعِ (obey) in the ayah

steps.m: the rejecters/deniers (plural, acted upon by تُطِعِ)
steps.n: Makes the word definite by adding ال and adds final vowel mark to show it's being acted upon
Changed from basic root to past tense verb meaning 'he pretended'
Changes from a verb (the act) to a noun (the person) who pretends/acts two-faced
Added 'Al' at the start to mean 'the' - makes it specific
Changes from one hypocrite to many hypocrites by adding ين at the end
The و (wa) at the start means 'and', joining 'the hypocrites' with 'the disbelievers' mentioned earlier
Adding sukun (ْ) on last letter makes it a command
Changes from verb 'to harm' into the noun 'harm' by adding ى
Added superscript alif (ٰ) to indicate long vowel sound
Added هُمْ at the end to show the harm belongs to them or affects them
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at start and doubling the middle letter shows doing the action to/for oneself
Adding و connects this word to what came before it, meaning 'and'
Adding ْ on the last letter makes it a command
This is a primary preposition that shows something is resting or depending upon something else. Here it connects the verb وَتَوَكَّلْ (and rely/depend) to اللَّهِ (Allah), showing who to rely upon.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes it, and the kasra at the end shows it's being acted upon by the word before it (على).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. The ى at the end is because this is a special type of word that ends in ي.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous part of the sentence

steps.m: and he was enough (subject of بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound. This word connects with بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا to mean 'Allah is sufficient as a guardian'
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one true God, not just any deity
Added fatha on the shadda for proper pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: in/by/with Allah (object of وَتَوَكَّلْ - and rely)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show reliance upon Allah, connecting it to the verb وَتَوَكَّلْ (and rely)

steps.m: to trust, rely upon, or put in charge

steps.n: Added ي between first and second root letters to show someone who does this action as their role. The vowels َ and ِ help shape the word pattern.

steps.m: a trustworthy guardian (object of كَفَىٰ - sufficient)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb كَفَىٰ (is sufficient) in the phrase 'and Allah is sufficient as a guardian'

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: The root letters combine with a shadda (doubled letter) to make a question word meaning 'which'
Added يٰٓ to make this a call for attention (like saying 'hey' to someone)
Added a small u-sound (damma) to make it into 'O you' for calling someone's attention
Added ها to make it address someone specifically
This is the base form used for relative pronouns, adding ي makes it able to connect to what comes after it
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people or things
Adding ال with shadda makes it point to specific people - in this case, those who believe, as shown by the next word ءَامَنُوٓا۟ (they believed)
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from just trusting to having complete faith
Added وا at the end to show many people (all men or mixed group) did this in the past
This is the basic form of the word that shows a point in time

steps.m: when/whenever (introducing a condition in نَكَحْتُمُ - you marry)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it conditional, connecting it to what happens next. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when you marry believing women.
Basic three-letter root meaning marriage
Added fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed final vowel to sukun to prepare for suffix
Added past tense marker ت to show completed action
Basic three-letter root about safety and trust
Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, with a hamza (ؤ) because the first root letter is أ
Added ٰت at the end to make it feminine plural. The ٰ (alif) is stretched to show it's a long sound.

steps.m: the believing women (who are being married)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ ('the') at the start and ِ at the end because this word is being acted upon in the sentence (the women are being married)

steps.m: then (connects this part about divorce to the previous part about marriage)
steps.n: This word connects two related ideas in sequence. Here it shows that the rules about divorce come after the rules about marriage. The ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) make the word stronger and clearer to pronounce.

steps.m: to set free, to release from bonds
steps.n: Basic three-letter root about releasing or setting free

Added shadda (ّ) on middle letter ل to show intensity, and fatha (َ) after first letter to show past tense. This changes the general meaning of 'release' to specifically mean 'divorce' in this context.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added مو to show multiple people did the action
Added هُنَّ as feminine plural object pronoun suffix meaning 'them'
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with قَبْلِ (before) to mean 'from before'
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: before (of أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ - that you touch them)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (أَن - that). Together they mean 'before that you touch them'
Basic particle that connects this clause to what came before it, showing what happened before touching/contact
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The ت at the start shows the person is both doing and receiving the action
Doubling the س shows the action is stronger or more intense
The و at the end shows this is about multiple males doing the action
Changed from 'they' to 'you all' and added هُنَّ (hunna) at the end to show you are touching multiple females
Basic interrogative word that asks about things or amounts
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. Here it connects to عِدَّةٍ (waiting period) to ask 'then what waiting period?'
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to show it's connected to what follows
Added the attached pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all' when attached to the end of words
Basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when pronouns are attached
Added هِ which shows this affects 'him/it'
Added نَّ to make it refer to multiple females

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects قَبْلِ (before) to the previous part of the sentence.

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it. The sukun (ْ) on ن shows the word ends here.

steps.m: to count or keep track of something
steps.n: Basic root about counting
Changes the root into a specific noun meaning a period of counting/waiting
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is any waiting period (not a specific one)

steps.m: to count or keep track of something
steps.n: Basic root about counting
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show basic past tense meaning
Added ة and ُ to make it a feminine noun meaning 'a count/period'
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: you all count it (referring to the waiting period عِدَّةٍ mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ها to show what is being counted, referring back to the waiting period mentioned earlier
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result of what came before. Connected letters with basic past tense vowels.
Changed from past tense 'he provided' to a command 'you all provide' by adding shadda and changing the vowels
Added و to show this is addressed to multiple people (you all)
Added هُنَّ to show this command affects female objects (them)
Basic root about free movement
Added shadda (double letter) to show doing the action with extra force/emphasis
Changed to command form for multiple people
Adds 'them' (feminine) as the object of the command
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Basic root about free movement
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to form verbal noun (releasing instead of he released)

steps.m: a releasing/divorce (as manner of doing it)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the divorce should be done (in a releasing way)
Basic root about beauty and grace
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into an adjective describing the quality

steps.m: beautifully (describes how سَرِّحُوهُنَّ/release them is done)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the action of releasing should be done - with beauty/grace
This is the basic vocative particle used to call attention to someone
The alif is written as a long vowel mark above the يا for style
أيّ is added to make the call more specific, like saying 'O you who...'
هَا makes the call address a group rather than individual
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun

steps.m: prophet (one who brings news from Allah)
steps.n: Changed from general 'news' to mean someone who delivers divine news
Adding ال makes the noun definite
Added ُ to show this is doing the action in the sentence (the subject of 'wanted/أَرَادَ')
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'verily' in English
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to أَحْلَلْنَا (we have made lawful) in this ayah.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) when it comes at the end of a word, but this doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added أ at start to show causing something to happen, and sukun on first letter
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and sukun before it
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك with fatha to show this is directed to a single male person (you). In this ayah, it's specifically addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Added vowel marks to make the basic singular noun form. The fatha and sukun pattern turns the action into a person/thing.
This is a broken plural form, meaning it changes the inside of the word instead of adding an ending

steps.m: your spouses (object of أَحْلَلْنَا - we made lawful)
steps.n: Adding second person singular possessive pronoun suffix. This word is the direct object (what is being made lawful) of the verb أحللنا (We have made lawful).
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used for feminine plural subjects. It shows we're talking about multiple women or feminine things.
Adding ٱلْ with a shadda makes it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'. The ى shows it's for feminine plural.
The small alif (ٓ) at the end helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
Basic meaning of giving or bringing something to someone
Changes from basic 'bring' to 'cause to bring/give'
Changed the final ى to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the past tense suffix
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' (masculine singular) did the action in the past
The root forms the plural noun meaning 'wages' or 'rewards'
Makes the word plural to show multiple rewards or payments
Added fatha to show this is the object receiving the action
Added هُنَّ to show these wages belong to multiple females
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it. This word connects to مَلَكَتْ (possessed) in the ayah.
This root carries the basic meaning of having control or ownership over something
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added تْ at the end to show a female did this action. This word is connected to يَمِينُكَ (your right hand) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'to be fortunate' to noun 'right side' which was considered blessed/fortunate
Added damma to show this is the subject of مَلَكَتْ (possessed/owned)
Added possessive pronoun 'your' (masculine singular). This word is what is possessed in the phrase مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ (what your right hand possessed).
Starting with the base particle ما which refers to what Allah has given as spoils of war
Added مِن (from) at the beginning. The ن combines with م to make a stronger م sound (shown by ّ)
The final ا is written with a special mark (آ) to show it should be pronounced longer

steps.m: to give back/return as a gift

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas.
Added أَ at the beginning to show Allah is causing the action to happen

steps.m: he caused to return/bestowed (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The long alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's both long and has a hamza. This word is connected to اللَّهُ which comes after it, showing that Allah is the one doing the bestowing.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Arabic.
Added the ُ ending to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. The root letters ع-ل-و/ي relate to being high or elevated.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'upon you'. This is like adding 'you' to make it clear who is being talked about.
Root meaning 'to build' changed to make the feminine word 'daughter' by adding ت
Changed from singular 'daughter' to plural 'daughters' using the feminine plural ending

steps.m: and daughters (of عَمِّكَ - your paternal uncle)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning and the genitive case ending because this word is connected to 'your paternal uncle' in the verse
Added kasra and كَ to show possession. This word is connected to بَنَاتِ (daughters) in the phrase 'daughters of your paternal uncle'
Removed كَ (meaning 'your') from the end of 'uncle'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the base word
Adding ات makes it a feminine plural noun meaning daughters
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is connected to the word after it (عَمِّكَ - your paternal uncle)
The و connects this to the previous items in the list of people being described
The root ع-م-م becomes عَمَّة (paternal aunt) first, then gets ات at the end to make it mean 'multiple aunts'. It belongs to بَنَاتِ (daughters) in the sentence.
Adding كَ (ka) at the end means 'your' - like saying 'your aunts' instead of just 'aunts'. This word is connected to 'daughters of' in the verse.
Root meaning 'build' changed to mean 'daughters' by adding ات to make it plural feminine
Added وَ (and) at beginning and ِ at end to show possession

steps.m: to care for family members, protect

steps.n: Forms active participle pattern فَاعِل meaning 'one who cares for family'. In Arabic culture, maternal uncles have a special role in caring for and protecting their sisters' children.
The كَ at the end shows it belongs to you or is connected to you
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding fathas and making the last letter an alif
Added ات to make it mean multiple females
The ِ ending shows this word belongs to or is connected to something that comes after it
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added vowels to make the word for 'maternal uncle' - someone who cares for family from the mother's side
Added ة to make the feminine form, changing 'uncle' to 'aunt'
Added ات to make the plural form for 'aunts'
Changed to show these are 'someone's maternal aunts' and ends with -i sound because it follows 'daughters of'
Added كَ to show the aunts belong to 'you' (masculine singular)
This is the basic feminine singular relative pronoun. It's a fundamental grammatical word that doesn't come from a root.
Adding ا changes this from referring to one feminine thing to referring to many feminine things. In this ayah, it refers to 'those (women) whom' when talking about the Prophet's wives.
Changes from verb (to migrate) to noun (migrator) by adding ا after first letter

steps.m: they (feminine plural) migrated (with the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: The نَ ending shows this refers to multiple women who migrated. In this ayah, it specifically refers to the female relatives who migrated with the Prophet ﷺ.
This is a basic particle meaning 'with' - it's not derived from a root
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'with you'
Adds ة to make it clearly feminine
Adds two small lines on top (tanween) to mean 'a woman' and show it's being acted upon
Adds conjunction prefix to connect to previous phrase
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'believed' into 'believer' - someone who does the believing
Adds ة to make it refer to a woman who believes

steps.m: a believing woman (object of يَسْتَنكِحَهَا - that he may marry her)
steps.n: Adding tanween fatha (ً) shows this word is the object being acted upon in the sentence. Here it refers to the believing woman that the Prophet ﷺ may marry.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible scenario. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if the Prophet wants' (إِنْ أَرَادَ)

steps.m: to give freely as a gift

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it 'he gave' - like adding an 'ed' in English to show it happened in the past

steps.m: she gave as a gift (to النَّبِيِّ)
steps.n: The suffix ت shows the subject is 'she' in this context. This woman is offering herself as a gift to the Prophet.
Adding the basic vowel marks to show it's a past tense verb
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added fatha at the end because this word is being possessed by something after it
Added هَا at the end to show this is referring to 'her self'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Adding يّ changes it from 'news' to 'one who brings news'
Added ال to specify we're talking about a particular prophet, and added shadda on the ن because it comes after ال
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is for/to the prophet

steps.m: if (introduces condition for وَهَبَتْ - she offered)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible scenario. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if she offered herself' that connects to what comes after.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at start makes the verb transitive (can take an object)

steps.m: to inform or bring important news

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make it mean 'news' instead of 'to inform'
Changed the middle vowel and added ي to show someone who does this action
Changed hamza to shadda for easier pronunciation
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن because it comes after ال
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb أَرَادَ (he wanted) in this ayah

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the Prophet's desire (أَرَادَ) with the action of marrying (يَسْتَنكِحَهَا). It works like 'that' in English when you say 'he wanted that he marry her.'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding 'است' at the start shows someone is seeking or requesting something

steps.m: he seeks to marry her (object of أَرَادَ/he wanted)
steps.n: Added 'ها' to show the woman being referred to. This whole phrase tells us what the Prophet wanted to do.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed from verb (being pure) to noun (one who is pure) by adding alif

steps.m: exclusively (describing the marriage permission in يَسْتَنكِحَهَا)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to show this describes HOW the marriage permission works (exclusively/purely)
Added fatha to show basic preposition meaning
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: surely for you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك with fatha to show it's specifically for 'you' (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this verse)
This is a basic preposition showing the starting point or source of something

steps.m: from/of (connecting دُونِ to what comes before)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) to show this is connecting to the word دُونِ (besides/other than) that comes after it in the ayah
Adding damma (ُ) changes the word into a common preposition meaning 'besides' or 'without'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to the next word الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the believers). Together they mean 'besides/other than the believers'.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling secure
Changed from 'felt safe' to 'one who believes' - shows someone who has faith/trust
Added ending to show multiple believers
Added ال to specify these particular believers. The word comes after دُونِ (besides) in the ayah.
This is a non-derived particle that adds emphasis or indicates completion of an action
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. In this ayah, قَدْ emphasizes that Allah certainly knew what He prescribed, connecting to the verb عَلِمْنَا (we knew).
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Adding نا changes it from 'he knew' to 'we knew'

steps.m: that which (connects مَلَكَتْ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a connecting word (relative pronoun) that links مَلَكَتْ (possessed) to what comes before it. In this ayah, it's used to add another category to what is permissible.

steps.m: to make something required or necessary

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: we made (something) required (referring to marriage rules in this ayah)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end and changed the middle vowel to sukun (ْ) to show 'we' did the action
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَزْوٰجِهِمْ (their spouses) to show what was prescribed for them.
Added a long vowel mark (ٓ) to show the extended pronunciation at the end of the word
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the singular noun for one spouse
Changed from singular 'spouse' to plural 'spouses' by adding أَ at start and ا after و - this is a common plural pattern in Arabic
Added هِمْ at the end to show possession (their)
This is a relative pronoun that introduces a clause describing something. Here it connects to the verb مَلَكَتْ (possessed/owned) to mean 'what was possessed'.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at the end to show a female did the action. This word is connected to يَمِينُكَ (your right hand) in this ayah.
Forms the noun for 'right hand/side' - this meaning comes from the ancient belief that the right side was blessed
Changes from 'right hand' to its plural form by adding extra letters - like how we make 'foot' into 'feet' but in Arabic
Adds 'their' (هُمْ) to the word 'right hands', making it 'their right hands'. Often means things they own.
This particle shows purpose or reason
Adding كَيْ strengthens the meaning of purpose

steps.m: so that not/in order that not (connected to يَكُونَ)
steps.n: Adding لَا makes the purpose negative. This compound particle connects to the verb يَكُونَ (it be) to show the purpose of preventing something.
This is the basic concept of existing or being
Added يَ at the beginning to show present/future tense and who is doing the action
Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is in the subjunctive mood
This is a preposition (عَلَى) combined with the pronoun suffix كَ (you)
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning the result of being restricted
The double damma (tanween) shows this is indefinite - meaning 'any' difficulty. This word is what would not be upon the Prophet according to the verse.
Added fatha marks to show it happened in the past. The و changes to ا because it's in the middle of the word - this is a common change that makes it easier to say.
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to the previous part, showing that Allah being forgiving is connected to what came before
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject

steps.m: to cover up faults/sins with forgiveness
one who forgives again and again
steps.n: Added و after second letter and vowels to show this describes someone who forgives repeatedly
Added ًا at the end because this word is used as a description (adjective) in the sentence

steps.m: to show kindness and care for others

steps.n: Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ي makes it describe someone who has this quality very strongly
Adding ًا shows this word is being used as an object in the sentence
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he delayed' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you put off/delay (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed يُ to تُ to show 'you' instead of 'he'. This refers to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ being able to postpone time with some of his wives.
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to whichever of the Prophet's wives he chooses to defer.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters are connected with vowels. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added ا in middle to show this is a special type of verb with a 'hollow' middle
Basic preposition indicating source or origin
Adds basic singular masculine/neutral pronoun
Changes the pronoun to feminine plural by replacing the masculine هُ with هُنَّ
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he sheltered' to present tense 'you shelter' by adding تُ
Added 'wa' (and) at the beginning. The long vowel at the end shows this action is done to someone else.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
When adding a pronoun after إِلَى, the ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect with the next part

steps.m: to you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added كَ which means 'you' to show who the action is directed towards. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about arranging time with his wives.
This is a special word that refers to people. In this ayah, it's being used to talk about which wives the Prophet (peace be upon him) could defer spending time with.
Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel marks
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed last vowel to ُ to show present tense
Added آ to lengthen the vowel sound after ش
This is a basic word meaning 'whoever' that connects to the next word with kasra
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
This is the basic concept of seeking or wanting something
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added اِبْتَ at the beginning to show doing the action for oneself with effort
Changed to show 'you' did the action by adding تَ at the end, and added يْ before it because the word ends in ى
Combined two separate words: مِن (from) and مَن (whom/who)
The ن of مِن combines with the م of مَن to make a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark). This is like when we say 'immense' in English instead of 'in-mense'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for the suffix
Added تَ at the end to show that 'you' (masculine singular) did the action
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. In this ayah, it negates the word جُنَاحَ (blame), meaning 'then there is no blame on you'

steps.m: to lean, incline, or bend sideways
steps.n: This form changes the meaning from the action to a related noun

steps.m: sin or blame (because sin is seen as leaning away from right path)
steps.n: Added ُ at start and ا after ن to make it into a noun meaning 'sin' or 'blame', which comes from the idea of leaning away from the right path

steps.m: sin or blame (object of لَا meaning 'there is no')
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of لَا (no/not). Together لَا جُنَاحَ means 'there is no sin/blame'.
The ى at the end changes to ي with sukun when something is attached to it. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added كَ at the end to show this applies to 'you' (singular). In this ayah, it's referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Base demonstrative pronoun with alif replaced by alif waslah for pronunciation
Added لِ to change 'this' to 'that' (showing something farther away)

steps.m: that (pointing to what was just mentioned)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're referring to something just mentioned in the previous part of the verse
Connected the root letters that mean being near or close
Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison (like adding '-er' in English). The و at the end changed to ى because it's easier to pronounce.
Added an alif mark (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation

This is a connecting word that links two ideas together. Here it connects the previous statement to what happens next (that their eyes become cool/content). It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I hope that you are happy.'
Basic meaning refers to something becoming settled or cool, often used for comfort and contentment
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger - like settling very firmly
Adding ت shows the settling is happening by itself, not someone settling something else
Forms the broken plural of عين (eye)
Added the vowel marks 'fatha' and 'sukun' to make the noun meaning 'eye'
Changes 'eye' to its plural form for small numbers (3-10 eyes)
Added damma at end as connector for following pronouns
Added feminine plural possessive pronoun 'their' at the end
This is a basic negation word that stops something from happening. Here it's stopping the sadness mentioned in يَحْزَنَّ (they become sad).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about making their eyes happy.
This is the basic concept of feeling sadness
Adding fatha and kasra vowels creates the past tense form
Adding يَ at the start and changing the vowels turns 'he felt sad' into 'he feels/will feel sad'

steps.m: he should feel sad (in context: they should not feel sad)
steps.n: Adding the shadda makes it a command/suggestion (like 'should' or 'must'), which with لا means 'should not' or 'must not'
Basic meaning of being content or satisfied with something
The root letter و at the end changes to ى when vowels are added to make the word easier to say
Adding يَ at the beginning shows this is happening in the present
The ى changes to يْ and نَ is added at the end to show multiple females doing the action
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'
The alif is lengthened for emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how something was done. In this ayah, it refers to what was given to the wives.
Added alif after hamza to make the causative form, meaning to make something/someone come/arrive
Changed from basic 'to give' to 'you gave' by adding تَ at the end
Added feminine plural object pronoun to show who received the giving
Added damma and shadda to create the word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject
Added هُنَّ at the end to show this refers to 'all of them (feminine plural)'
This is a unique proper name that doesn't follow normal word patterns. It's the name of God in Arabic.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: God (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: and God (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it
This is the basic meaning of knowing or having knowledge
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now, and ُ at the end to show it's in the basic form. The ْ (sukun) helps with pronunciation. This word describes what Allah does in this ayah.

steps.m: with what (relates to يَرْضَيْنَ - they are pleased)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يَرْضَيْنَ (they are pleased). In this context, it shows what the women are pleased with - specifically what was given to them.
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it shows location within 'your hearts'.
This is the basic concept the word comes from - hearts are named this way because they can change direction (physically and metaphorically)
Forms the singular noun 'heart' from the basic concept of turning
Changes the word to show multiple hearts by adding و between letters and changing vowels
Adds kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something
Adds كُمْ to show these hearts belong to multiple people being spoken to
This shows the basic meaning of existing or being in a state
Added fatha marks and changed the middle weak letter و to ا to show past tense

steps.m: and he was (connected to يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This word connects to يَعْلَمُ (knows) in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Added 'the' to make it definite

steps.m: The One True God (subject of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: This is a special word that comes from 'the god' but changes form completely to become Allah - the name of The One True God. The U sound at the end shows He is doing the action.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed to pattern فَعِيل which shows this is a permanent, intense quality
Added tanween (two dashes) and alif at the end to make it describe Allah in this sentence
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together
Forms intensive adjective pattern فَعِيل indicating 'one who has a lot of this quality'

steps.m: very gentle/patient (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this an adjective describing Allah, showing His gentleness and patience
This is a basic negative particle used to negate what follows it. In this ayah, it negates يَحِلُّ (is permissible), making the meaning 'is not permissible'.
Adding shadda (ّ) makes the negation stronger. This emphasizes that something is definitely not allowed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ makes it present tense
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter ل

steps.m: for/to you (speaking to one male) (connected to يَحِلُّ - is permissible)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك with fatha to show it means 'you' (when speaking to one male). This word connects to يَحِلُّ (is permissible) to show what is not permissible for the person being addressed.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make the basic singular form
The word for 'woman' gets an extra 'ا' (alif) to make it mean 'women' - this is a special plural form
Added ال and doubled the ن with shadda because it comes after ال
Changed alif to small alif (ٱ), lengthened the alif before hamza (آ), and added damma for nominative case
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects the word أَزْوٰجٍ (spouses) to تَبَدَّلَ (exchange), showing what is being exchanged.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added small ن at the end to show a special pronunciation when it's followed by ب

steps.m: to be distant or come after something

steps.n: Root letters ب-ع-د got vowels (َ and ْ) to become the common word 'after'
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added elongation mark (آ) to show the long vowel sound is emphasized
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Basic particle that connects parts of a sentence. Here it connects to the verb تَبَدَّلَ (exchange/substitute) to show what is not allowed.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something a lot or with intensity
Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action is being done to or by oneself
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with'
Added the basic pronoun ه (him/it) to show who the preposition affects
Added نَّ to change the pronoun from singular masculine (him) to plural feminine (them)
Basic form of the preposition
Added kasra under م and sukun on ن to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, مِنْ connects the word أَزْوٰجٍ (spouses) to النِّسَآءُ (the women), showing which women are being referred to.

steps.m: to pair up or join together

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make the noun meaning 'spouse' - like turning 'pair' into 'partner'
Changes from single spouse to multiple spouses by adding أَ at the start and ا in the middle - this is a common plural pattern
Added tanwin kasra at the end because it comes after مِنْ (from/of). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This word introduces a hypothetical situation.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Adding أ at the beginning makes the meaning causative - changing from feeling amazed to making others feel amazed
Adding the vowels makes it a proper past tense verb

steps.m: he/it impressed you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows who was impressed. In this ayah, it's referring to what impresses the Prophet ﷺ about potential wives.
Root letters got vowels (damma and sukun) to make a simple noun meaning 'beauty'
Added damma ending to show this is the main subject

steps.m: their beauty (referring to the women mentioned)
steps.n: Added هُنَّ to show the beauty belongs to multiple females (referring back to النِّسَآءُ/women mentioned earlier)
This is a basic particle used to show exception - it tells us that what comes after it is different from what was mentioned before
Added the vowel mark kasra (ِ) under the إ and shadda (ّ) on ل to show how it's pronounced
Basic form of the relative pronoun used for non-human things. In this ayah, it connects to مَلَكَتْ to mean 'what/that which you possess'
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show the subject is feminine (referring to يَمِينُكَ - your right hand)
Added vowels (fatHa, kasra, damma) to the root letters to make the word mean 'right hand/side'

steps.m: your right hand (possessed by you, subject of مَلَكَتْ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show possession. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb مَلَكَتْ (possessed) in this ayah.
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he was' by adding fathas and turning و into ا
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added ُ to show Allah is the subject doing the action in كَانَ (was). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else. It's like saying 'on' in English.
Added the small alif (ٰ) which is just a writing style - it doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'every' or 'all'. This word describes شَىْءٍ (thing) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it
The root letters get vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make the basic noun meaning 'thing'. The hamza (ء) stays at the end.
Added the genitive case marker because this word is possessed by كُلِّ (every/all). The ي is written as ى when followed by hamza.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'watched' to noun meaning 'one who watches intensively'

steps.m: an All-Watchful One (describing Allah as the object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this describes Allah as a watcher (connected to كَانَ)
This is a vocative particle combined with a demonstrative
The damma ending makes it ready to connect to what comes next
Adds يا at the start to call out to someone, like saying 'Hey!' or 'O!'
Adds ها to make it clear we're talking to multiple people, like saying 'O you all!'
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun and changing it to the relative form
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to someone or something being described
Adding vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to a group of people instead of just one person. In this ayah, it's describing the believers who are being addressed.
Added alif after first letter to change meaning from just feeling safe to actively believing
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَدْخُلُوا۟ (enter) into 'do not enter'. It's one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Root letters put together with short 'a' vowels to make the word for house
Changed middle vowel to sukun to make the basic noun form
Changed from singular 'house' to plural by adding و and changing the middle vowels to u-sounds (dumma)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb تَدْخُلُوا۟ (you enter)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changes from 'news' to 'news-bringer' by adding ي and doubling the last letter
Adds definite article to make the noun definite
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Basic exception particle that shows something is being excluded from what was mentioned before
Added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the sound and add emphasis to the exception
The alif is lengthened to آ to add even more emphasis when reading

steps.m: that (connects to يُؤْذَنَ - you are permitted)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it connects the main idea (entering) with its condition (being permitted). It's like saying 'except that you are permitted' in English.
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he permitted'

steps.m: to be permitted (passive form of أَذِنَ)
steps.n: Changed to passive form by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This shows the permission is being received rather than given.
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to connect it with what follows
Added the pronoun كم (you all) with damma to show it's connected to the preposition ل
This is a preposition that connects words by showing direction or destination. Here it connects to the word طَعَامٍ (food) to show what they're being invited to.
The alif at the end (ىٰ) is written with a small alif above it. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the root into a noun meaning the thing that is eaten

steps.m: a food (following the word إِلَىٰ meaning 'to')
steps.n: The ٍ shows this follows the word إِلَىٰ (to) and means 'a' instead of 'the'

steps.m: to change or be different from something

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something being different or other than something else
Added a fatha at the end to show this word is describing نٰظِرِينَ (those waiting). Both words have the same ending mark because one describes the other.
Adding vowels to make the basic verb pattern

steps.m: ones who watch/observe (referring to believers being addressed)
steps.n: Makes it plural masculine. In this ayah, it describes the believers who shouldn't linger watching food being prepared at the Prophet's house.

steps.m: to reach the right time or become ripe

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to ripen' into a noun meaning 'the right time'
Adds possession marker to show whose time it is
This is a basic particle that shows contrast with what came before. The alif after ل makes the word stronger.
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement to what came before it, like saying 'and yet' or 'and however' in English.

steps.m: when (connecting to دُعِيتُمْ - you were invited)
steps.n: This is a time word that connects two events. Here it connects being invited (دُعِيتُمْ) with what should happen at that time. It's like saying 'at the time when you were invited'.
Changed the root letters د-ع-و into a past tense verb by adding fathas, with و changing to ا at the end
Changed from 'he called' to 'was called' by changing the vowels - put a u-sound on first letter and i-sound on second letter
Added تُمْ to show this happened to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he entered' to 'enter!' - a command telling someone to go in
Makes the command plural - for multiple people

steps.m: so all of you enter! (after being invited)
steps.n: Adds فَ to show this is what you should do after being invited, as mentioned in the previous part of the verse
This is a conditional particle indicating time. It shows when something happens.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. In this ayah, it connects the condition of finishing eating (طَعِمْتُمْ) with what should happen next.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to taste'
Adding تَ at the end changes it from 'he tasted' to 'you tasted'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added reflexive pattern to show the action is done to oneself

steps.m: spread yourselves out! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed to command form for multiple people
Added فَ to show this command comes after the previous action (eating) mentioned in the ayah
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْذَنَ (being permitted) in this ayah
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this negative statement with what came before it

steps.m: to feel comfortable or familiar with something

steps.n: The است prefix shows seeking or wanting the basic meaning - like wanting to feel comfortable
The مُ prefix and ِ vowel change it from an action to someone doing the action

steps.m: ones (plural men) who are seeking comfort/familiarity (with the Prophet's presence)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it plural for men. In this verse, it refers to people lingering too long in the Prophet's house, feeling too comfortable there.
Added vowels and ي to make it into a noun meaning the speech or conversation itself
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose and tanween at end to show it's indefinite
Basic form of the emphatic particle that strengthens statements
Added kasra under the alif to show correct pronunciation
Added shadda to strengthen the emphasis. This particle introduces a statement that the speaker wants to emphasize as important or certain.
This is a demonstrative pronoun for something distant, with a plural masculine addressee suffix
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas, which is a common pattern in Arabic
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he harmed' in the past
Changed from past tense (أَذَى) to present tense (يُؤْذِي) by adding يُ at the start. The word shows ongoing harm directed at the Prophet.

steps.m: someone who tells important news from Allah
steps.n: Changed the word to show the person who does the action, like changing 'teach' to 'teacher'
Changed the ء to ّ (shadda) to make it stronger and easier to say
Added ال at the beginning to mean 'the', and the first letter gets a shadda because it starts with ن
Changed the beginning alif to a special type (ٱ) that's used when connecting words
Changed the ending to show this word is being affected by the verb يُؤْذِى (bothers/annoys)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Form X (استفعل) shows entering into a state or seeking something
Changed to present tense by replacing initial alif with ya
Added فَ (so/then) to connect with previous action. The final ى with small ي (ۦ) is a Quranic writing style for this word.
Basic preposition indicating source or origin

steps.m: from you all (referring to the believers being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adds second person masculine plural pronoun suffix to show who is being addressed. This connects to the earlier part of the verse where Allah is addressing the believers.
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it
Changed regular alif to wasl alif (ٱ) since it comes after و
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَسْتَحْىِۦ (is shy) into its opposite meaning (is not shy). It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Added letters at the beginning to show seeking or feeling something within oneself

steps.m: he feels shy (from يُؤْذِى - being bothered)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show this is happening now and the subject is 'he'. This word shows the Prophet's reaction to being bothered (يُؤْذِى) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: he feels shy (from يُؤْذِى - being bothered)
steps.n: Changed the writing style for the final ي according to Quranic writing rules, but pronunciation stays the same
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or showing the starting point of something
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word الْحَقِّ (the truth). This makes the words flow together more smoothly when reading.
Root letters ح-ق-ق became a noun حَقّ with a shadda, changing from 'being true' to 'truth' itself
Added ال to show we're talking about absolute truth, not just any truth. This word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah.
The kasra (ِ) shows this word comes after مِنَ (from), like saying 'from the truth'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition. It connects to the following verb سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ (you asked them)
The و connects this clause to the previous instructions about entering the Prophet's houses

steps.m: to ask for something or request information

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he asked' to 'I asked' by adding تُ at the end
Changed تُ (I) to تُم (you all) to show many people asking instead of one person
Added و (him) to show who was being asked
Added هُ to show 'him' - the person being asked
Added نَّ to change 'him' to 'them (feminine)'
Transforms the action into a concrete thing that gives the benefit
The ًا ending shows this is what's being asked for in the phrase 'when you ask them for something.' The double marks (tanwin) show it's any general item, not a specific one.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
This changes the verb to show that multiple males performed the action of asking in the past.
Adds the masculine singular object pronoun 'him' to show who was asked
Changes the object from 'him' to 'them (feminine)' by adding نَّ

1. Added فَ at the beginning to mean 'so/then'
2. The hamza (ء) is written differently here due to its position in the word, but the pronunciation stays the same
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to وَرَآءِ (behind) to show where to ask from.

steps.m: to be in back of something

steps.n: Added alif and hamza to make a noun meaning 'behind' or 'back'
Added kasra to show this word connects to حِجَابٍ (screen/partition) that comes after it

steps.m: to block, screen, or prevent access

steps.n: Added the pattern that turns the action of screening into a physical barrier or partition. The ا after ج makes it into a noun meaning the thing that does the screening.

steps.m: a screen/partition (after مِن وَرَآءِ - from behind)
steps.n: The tanwin (ٍ) at the end shows this is any screen/partition (not a specific one). It has this ending because it comes after the words مِن وَرَآءِ (from behind).
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) for you all
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is being addressed to multiple people
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The أ prefix makes this a comparative/superlative form, like 'purer' or 'purest'

steps.m: to turn something over, to change the state of something

steps.n: Changed the word to mean many hearts by adding the ُ sound after ق and ل, and putting و between ل and ب. This is like how in English we add 's' to make things plural, but in Arabic we change the middle of the word.
Added -i ending to show this heart belongs to someone
Added لِ at the beginning to show these hearts are receiving the effect of being made purer (أَطْهَرُ)
The كُمْ suffix means 'your' when talking to multiple males
Connected the root letters with basic vowel pattern for a noun
Changes from one heart (قَلْب) to many hearts (قُلُوب) by changing the vowels inside the word
The هِنَّ suffix means 'their' when talking about multiple females
Adding the conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous phrase
This is a negative particle that negates the verb كَانَ (was) that comes after it in this ayah. It changes the meaning from 'was' to 'was not'
The وَ prefix means 'and'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he/it was (describes يُؤْذِى - was causing harm)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that happens automatically for pronunciation.
Starting with the preposition لَ which shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun كُمْ which refers to multiple people being addressed

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what was not allowed - harming the Prophet. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'It is not for you that you harm...'
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he harmed' - the alif with hamza became a long aa sound
Changed to present tense form
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by changing first letter
Changed to plural 'you all' form
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تُؤْذُوا (you harm/annoy)
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال and merged it with the word, making it a proper noun for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the first lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word رَسُولَ (Messenger) - showing it means 'Messenger of Allah'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to the previous statement in the ayah.
This is a foundational particle that introduces what comes after it as a complete thought or action. Here it connects to تُؤْذُوا (you harm/offend) to show what was not appropriate for them to do.

steps.m: to marry or form a marriage contract

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject by adding ت at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you (all) marry (as a prohibition with لَا)
steps.n: Added plural marker وا to show multiple people are addressed. The word appears with لَا to form a prohibition (do not marry)
Added vowel marks to make the basic singular noun form
This is a broken plural form, meaning it changes the inside of the word instead of adding an ending

steps.m: his wives (whom you may not marry)
steps.n: The word 'spouses' became 'his wives' by adding هُ (meaning 'his') at the end. The alif (ا) is written differently (ٰ) but sounds the same.
Basic preposition form meaning 'from'
Added kasra under meem to show it's a complete word, not just letters
Added a small noon mark (called qalqala) after the noon to show a special pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Changes from root letters to basic noun meaning 'after'
The word 'after' gets a small 'i' sound at the end because something comes after it (like saying 'after OF something')
Added pronoun 'him' to show whose 'after' it is
Added elongation mark to emphasize the 'him'
Connected the letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning endless time

steps.m: forever (describing how long something will not happen)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how long something will last. In this ayah, it describes how long it would not be allowed to marry the Prophet's wives after him.

Adding the shadda (double letter mark) changes this from a basic word meaning 'if/that' to one that adds emphasis to what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that 'this' (ذٰلِكُمْ) is something important.
This is the basic pointing word used to show something nearby
Adding ل with kasra (ِ) changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) for all of you
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is being said to multiple people (2 or more)
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'

steps.m: he was/existed (describes يُؤْذِى - was causing harm)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic for hollow verbs.
This word shows location or closeness in relation to Allah in this ayah
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This word is unique and specifically refers to the One True God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, as this word describes whose messenger (رَسُولَ) is being referred to. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.

steps.m: to be great/magnificent in size or status

steps.n: Added ي between the last two root letters and vowels to make it describe something with this quality, like changing 'greatness' to 'great'

steps.m: great/magnificent (describing how serious this matter was to Allah)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how serious the matter was in Allah's sight. This ending shows it's connected to the word كان (was) earlier in the verse.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - in this case, it connects to the verb تُبْدُوا۟ (you disclose) to show that something will happen if/when you disclose something
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from past tense 'he showed' to present tense 'you show' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowel
Changed the final weak letter from ي to و with damma for easier pronunciation
Added alif (ا) at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'thing'. This word is what's being revealed in the phrase 'if you reveal something'.
Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon (the thing being revealed). The hamza changes position due to the ending.
This is a connecting word that joins two possible actions: showing something openly or hiding it
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel marks
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Changed from 'you (singular) hide' to 'you all hide' by changing the ending vowel

steps.m: you all hide it (connected to تُبْدُوا through أَوْ/or)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what is being hidden. This pairs with تُبْدُوا (reveal) through أَوْ (or) - either reveal something or hide it.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more certain. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'surely' in English.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This word connects to ٱللَّهَ (Allah) which comes after it, emphasizing 'then surely Allah...'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The hamza is dropped in this special word.
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being discussed in this sentence
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's in the middle of the word - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how Allah is knowing - with/of every thing
Added kasra at the end because this word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters ش-ي-أ form the common noun شَيْء (thing) by adding a fatha on ش and a sukun on ي. This word is what is being revealed in the sentence.
Adding the genitive case ending (kasratan) makes it 'of a thing' - like saying 'of' in English
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed from 'he knew' (past action) to 'All-Knowing' (permanent quality) by using the pattern with ي
Added tanween alif to show this describes how Allah was (كَانَ) in this ayah
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
Adding shadda on ل makes the negative stronger, like saying 'definitely not' or 'not at all'. This emphasizes that there is absolutely no blame on them in this case.

steps.m: to lean or incline towards something

steps.n: Changes from basic root meaning to a noun describing a state of blame/sin
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of لا (no/not).
The preposition عَلَى changes its ending when connecting to pronouns. The ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect with endings.
Added هِ which means 'him/it' - this connects the word to what it's referring to
Added نَّ to show we're talking about multiple females. This appears in this verse because it's referring to the Prophet's wives.
