Basic preposition showing location or position within something
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to show this connects to what follows. Here it introduces 'their fathers' (ءَابَآئِهِنَّ).

steps.m: to care for as a father

steps.n: Changed from verb root meaning 'to father' into the simple word for 'father'
Changed to plural form by adding long alif after first letter and اء at end
Changed ending to show possession
Added feminine 'their' to show these are the women's fathers
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation of the negation particle
Extended the alif to show emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: and not (connecting to previous items in the list)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this item to the previous items in the list of people mentioned in the ayah
Root means 'to build/create' - changed to mean a person who was created/built (a son)
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple sons
Changed ending to show 'of' (possession) and added hamza at the end
Added feminine plural possessive pronoun to show these are the sons belonging to a group of women
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added negation particle لا with fatha to show it's connected to the previous و
Elongated the final alif to show emphasis in recitation. This word connects to أَبْنَآئِهِنَّ (their sons) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for 'brother'
Changed the word to show multiple brothers using the special plural pattern
Changed from regular plural 'brothers' to dual form 'two brothers' by changing the ending
Added هِنَّ to show the brothers belong to multiple women
This is the basic negative word that makes things 'not' happen. It's one of the most common words in Arabic.
Added 'and' to connect this word to the previous phrase. In this ayah, it's connecting this part to the previous list of relatives.
Added a fatha and long alif to help with pronunciation when stopping on this word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Root means 'to build' - becomes 'son' as someone built into/born into the family
Changes from 'son' to 'sons' by making it plural - like changing 'child' to 'children'
Shows this word is possessed by or belongs to something after it (in this case, their sisters)
Changes from verb to plural noun meaning brothers
The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is the object of a preposition
Changed from 'two brothers' to 'brothers of' by changing the alif to show ownership
Added هِنَّ to show possession by multiple females
This is a basic negation particle that makes something negative. In this ayah, it's used to say there's no blame regarding certain relatives.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase, like making a list of 'not this and not that'
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning
The root letters transform into the word for 'son', which is related to the concept of building/establishing a family
Adding the sukun makes it the common word for 'son'
Changes 'son' to 'sons' by adding أَ at the start and ا after ن - a common way to make plurals

steps.m: sons of (possessed by إِخْوٰنِهِنَّ - their brothers)
steps.n: Adds kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by 'their brothers' in the phrase 'sons of their brothers'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together
The و is written with an alif above it (ٰ) when it comes before certain endings
Added ت to make it feminine plural form
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِنَّ to show these sisters belong to a group of females ('their' feminine plural)
This is a negative particle that makes things 'not' happen
Added وَ (and) to connect this negation to the previous list of negations in the ayah
This root is about women and feminine things
Root letters n-s-w became the plural word for women by adding vowels and a long aa sound at the end
Changed to 'of women' by adding a kasra sound at the end
Added feminine plural possessive ending to show 'their'
This is a negative particle that means 'not' - in this ayah it negates 'their women' to say there is no blame regarding them
The و connects this to the previous items in the list of people there is no blame regarding
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces 'whatever they possess' or 'what they rightfully own', connecting to the verb مَلَكَتْ (possessed/owned)
This root represents the basic concept of possession or ownership
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added ت at the end to show a feminine subject did the action. This word connects to أَيْمٰنُهُنَّ (their right hands) in the ayah.
Added vowels to make a basic noun. In Arabic, 'right hand' became connected to 'oath' because people would shake right hands when making promises.
This makes the word plural in a special way by changing its internal structure
Added damma to show this is doing the action in the sentence
Added هُنَّ to show these belong to multiple women

steps.m: to guard against harm or evil

steps.n: Command form for a female to protect herself. The root و-ق-ي changes to اِتَّقِي by adding اِتَّ at the start and ي at the end. The doubled ت (with shadda) shows intensity.
Added و with fatha to mean 'and'. The alif gets a wasl mark (ٱ) because it comes after و.

steps.m: and protect yourselves (command for group of females)
steps.n: Added نَ at the end to show this is a command for many women, not just one
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being witnessed in this verse)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being witnessed in 'Indeed, Allah is over all things a Witness'
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like saying 'Listen! This is important!' before making a statement. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah was witness.
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object of اتَّقِينَ - fear/be mindful of)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon by the verb 'fear/be mindful of' that comes before it
Added fatha marks to show it's past tense and the subject is 'he' or 'it'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns it into a preposition that shows one thing is above or over another
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
The doubled ل (with shadda) makes the meaning stronger, showing it means completely all or every single one
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to the word after it (شَىْءٍ), meaning 'of every thing'
The root letters were given vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the common noun meaning 'thing'. The hamza sits on top at the end.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by كُلِّ (every/all). The ي is written with alif maqsura (ى) when followed by hamza.
Added ي between second and third letters and kasra on middle letter to show someone who witnesses regularly
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the object of كَانَ (was)
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the first letter to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda (ّ) to make the word stronger. This shows that what comes after it (that Allah and His angels) is being emphasized
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (shadda) on the ل and a mark at the end (fatha) to show it comes after the word 'indeed'

steps.m: to send messages, communicate divine will

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'send messages' into a noun meaning 'one who delivers messages from God'
Changes from single angel (malak) to plural angels (malaa'ik) - a special plural form
Adding feminine ending to complete the plural form
Adding possessive pronoun to show these are Allah's angels
Adding conjunction to connect with previous word (Allah)
Added shadda to show intensity of the action
Added يُ at the start to make it mean 'he sends' instead of 'sent' (changes past to present)

steps.m: they send blessings intensely (referring to Allah and His angels)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple subjects (Allah and His angels) doing the action
This is a preposition that shows something is directed towards something else. In this ayah, it shows that the blessings are being directed upon the Prophet ﷺ.
The doubled ي (yaa) shows this is their special role
Added ال to specify we're talking about THE prophet
The initial alif changes to a wasl form since it comes after another word
Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb يُصَلُّونَ (they send blessings upon)

steps.m: to point to, indicate, or draw attention to something

steps.n: Root letters أيي combined into أيّ with a shadda, making it a question word meaning 'which/what'
Added the vocative particle يٰٓ to call attention
Added damma to show this is in the nominative case

steps.m: O you (calling to those who believe)
steps.n: Added ها to specify who is being called
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers who are addressed)
steps.n: Adding ال at the beginning and a shadda on the ذ makes it more specific, pointing to a particular group. In this ayah, it refers to the believers who are being told to send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ
The alif at the beginning is written with a special mark (waslah) because it connects with the previous word in pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added a long 'aa' sound at the start to mean 'to make someone trust/believe'

steps.m: they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. The small mark after وا (۟) is just to show proper recitation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Doubles the middle letter to show intensity and drops the weak letter و

steps.m: you all send blessings! (command to believers regarding النَّبِىِّ)
steps.n: Adds وا to make it a command for a group. This is part of Allah's command to believers to send blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the preposition
Changed the final ى to ي with sukun to prepare for connecting with the pronoun
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show 'him' as the object of the preposition

steps.m: to be at peace, to be safe

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter to show intensity and changed vowels to make it a command
Added وا to make it a command for a group

steps.m: and all of you make peace! (intensively)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Adding the shadda makes the peace-giving more complete and thorough
Changes the verb into a noun showing the action itself
Adding tanween (-an) makes it an adverb showing how thoroughly/completely the peace is given

Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that the people who harm Allah and His messenger are definitely cursed.
Added the definite article ال with shadda on ذ to make it specific

steps.m: those who (subject of يُؤْذُونَ - they harm)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word introduces those who do the action of harming in the ayah.
Basic meaning of causing harm or injury
Adds يُ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, with dots under middle letter to make 'harms'

steps.m: they harm (those who harm اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end changes to و because of this. This word refers to those who harm Allah and His messenger.
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The One True God' by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being harmed in this verse)
steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis, fatha at end to show Allah is being affected by the action of harming, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from 'to send' into 'messenger' - someone whose job is delivering messages

steps.m: His messenger (object of يُؤْذُونَ - they harm)
steps.n: Added هُۥ (his) with a fatha before it to show this is being acted upon. This word is receiving the action of harming in the phrase 'those who harm Allah and His messenger'.
Added the conjunction prefix وَ (and) at the beginning of the word

steps.m: to curse or remove from mercy
steps.n: Basic meaning of cursing or condemning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added هُمُ at the end to show who was cursed. This word is connected to اللَّهُ (Allah) who is doing the cursing.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's in nominative case
This is a preposition that shows location. Here it connects with the word ٱلدُّنْيَا (worldly life) to show 'in the worldly life'
Basic meaning of nearness or proximity
The root د-ن-و meaning 'near' becomes دُنْيَا 'worldly life' by adding special patterns and ا at the end. The word دُنْيَا means 'nearest/lower life' because it's closer to us than the afterlife

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with وَٱلْءَاخِرَةِ - the hereafter)
steps.n: Makes the noun definite. In this ayah, it's contrasted with 'the hereafter' to show Allah's punishment spans both realms.

steps.m: to come after or be last
steps.n: Basic meaning of being late or coming after something
Root meaning 'after/last' became an active participle (doer) meaning 'one who is last/final'
Added ة to turn 'last/final' into a noun meaning 'the afterlife'
Added ال to make it specific - this is THE afterlife, not just any later life
Added و to connect this to الدنيا (this world) mentioned before it
Added ِ because it comes after في (in) in the phrase في الدنيا والآخرة (in this world and the afterlife)
Basic meaning of counting or preparing something
The middle letter gets doubled (shown by shadda) to emphasize the action
Adding أ at beginning shows the action is being done to affect something else (in this case, preparing something for the people mentioned)

steps.m: and he prepared (for them a humiliating punishment - لَهُمْ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect with previous statement about Allah cursing them. The full meaning shows what was prepared.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them'. This refers back to 'those who harm Allah and His Messenger' mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added alif after second letter to make it a noun meaning the result of the action
Added tanween fatha at the end to show this is the object being prepared in the phrase 'prepared for them a punishment'
Basic meaning of being insignificant or contemptible
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'humiliating/degrading' (like something that makes others feel low)
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being used as an object or description

The letter م has a shaddah (doubled) in the after word, but the notes don't mention this change. Better notes would be: 'Letter م is doubled (has shaddah) and the word has tanween fatHa to show it describes the punishment (عَذَابًا) in the ayah'
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to things
Changed from pointing at something to describing someone/something specific
Changed from singular to plural to talk about multiple people
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يُؤْذُونَ (they harm) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning of hurting or causing harm to someone
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic form
Added يُ at the start to make it mean 'he harms' (present tense). The أ changed to ؤ because it comes after the ُ sound

steps.m: they harm (referring to those who harm believers)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those who harm the believers mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: to trust, to feel secure, to believe
steps.n: This root is about safety and belief
Changed the word to show someone who believes or has faith. The مُ at the start and the vowel marks show this is a person who does the action.
Added ين to show we're talking about many believers. In this verse, these believers are the ones being harmed by others.
Added ال (the) to show we're talking about a specific group of believers who are being harmed

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure
steps.n: This root is about safety and belief
Changed to active participle form showing the doer of the action
Changes from singular (one who believes) to multiple feminine believers
Shows the word is affected by a preposition or possession
Added ال to make it specific/definite - 'the believing women' rather than just any believing women
Added conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning of the word to connect it to previous phrase
This root is about being different or other than something
Root letters were connected and given vowels (َ ْ) to make a word meaning 'other than' - it's owned by ما in this ayah
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how something is done
Added kasra at the end because it's being possessed by the word after it (ما)
This is a special word that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it means 'that which' and links to the word اكْتَسَبُوا (they earned/committed) to show 'that which they committed'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to show someone doing something for themselves
Changed to show multiple people did this in the past
This is a special word used to emphasize that something definitely happened. Here it emphasizes the verb ٱحْتَمَلُوا۟ (they have borne)
Added فَ at the beginning to connect it to the previous statement, showing this is a result or consequence
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by a word starting with a hamza (ٱحْتَمَلُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added letters to show doing something to oneself (like carrying a burden)

steps.m: they took upon themselves (subject of فَقَدِ)
steps.n: Changed from singular (he) to plural (they) to show multiple people taking upon themselves. Connected to فَقَدِ (then certainly) to emphasize their action.
This root is about false accusations that leave one speechless
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes to a noun meaning 'intense/major false accusation'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being carried in احْتَمَلُوا (they have taken upon themselves)
This root is about doing something wrong or sinful
Converting from root concept to concrete noun meaning 'sin'

steps.m: a sin (object of احْتَمَلُوا/they have taken on)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is something being carried/taken on by the people mentioned in the verb احْتَمَلُوا (they have taken on)
Added 'and' to connect it with the previous word
This root is about being clear or obvious
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'something/someone that does the action' - like changing 'he made clear' to 'making clear'
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound

steps.m: clearly, manifestly (describing how they carried a burden in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا (an) at the end to make it describe HOW something happened, like adding -ly in English (clear → clearly)

steps.m: to point to or call attention to something
to strongly point to or call attention
steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Changed the ending vowel mark to show this word is asking 'which/who' as the doer of an action
Added يَا at the beginning to make it a call of attention
Added ها to make it refer to a group being addressed
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun
Changed the middle vowel and added ي to show someone who does this action
The hamza changed to a shadda for easier pronunciation
Added ال to point to a specific prophet and doubled the ن
Added ُ to show this is who is being spoken to in يٰٓأَيُّهَا (O you)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Removing the final vowel. This is telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to his wives, daughters, and the believing women.

steps.m: to pair or join together as spouses

steps.n: Root letters were given vowels to make the noun meaning 'spouse' or 'partner'
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and ا after second root letter
Added preposition لِ meaning 'to/for'
Added kasra at end to show genitive case because word follows preposition لِ

steps.m: to/for your spouses (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ك (ka) at the end to mean 'your' and has a small line (alif maqsurah) instead of regular alif
Root meaning 'build' changes to mean 'daughters' by adding feminine plural ending -aat
Added ك with fatha to show this means 'your daughters' - this connects to the earlier command قُل (say/tell) in the ayah, showing these are the Prophet's daughters being referred to
Added conjunction 'and'
Root meaning 'female' was turned into the broken plural 'women' by changing the pattern of vowels and adding alif
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to المؤمنين (the believers) that comes after it. Also lengthened the alif with a special mark (آ) for proper pronunciation.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowels to make it mean 'someone who believes/trusts' instead of the action itself
Added ين to show multiple believers
Added ال to specify these particular believers, and َ at the end because this word belongs to 'women' in the phrase 'women of the believers'
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern
Added يُ at the start to show it's happening now/future, and changed the vowels to show someone is making something else come near (rather than coming near themselves)
Added ي at the end since this is a weak verb ending in ي
Added نَ at the end to show this is about women doing the action. In this ayah, it's specifically about women bringing their outer garments closer to themselves.
Starting with the basic preposition على (upon) and adding vowel marks to show pronunciation
Changed the ending to prepare for connecting with the pronoun
Added هِ to show this applies to someone/something

steps.m: upon them (feminine) (connected to يُدْنِينَ - they should draw)
steps.n: Added نَّ to change it from singular masculine to plural feminine, showing this applies to a group of women. This connects to the verb يُدْنِينَ (they should draw) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing where the outer garments come from
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Forms the singular noun for a large outer garment that covers the whole body
Changes to plural form using the pattern فَعَالِيب to show multiple garments
Adds kasra at the end to prepare for possession
Adds feminine 'their' to show these garments belong to women
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
Added alif above the ذ to show it's a demonstrative
Added لِ to show the thing being pointed to is far away
Added كَ at the end to show we're talking about one masculine thing
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison. The ى at the end replaces the و because it's easier to pronounce. This word shows that wearing the outer garments makes it more likely for believing women to be recognized as Muslims.
Added alif mark (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: that (connects to يُعْرَفْنَ - they are recognized)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the previous idea (drawing close their outer garments) with its purpose (that they be recognized). It shows why the action is done.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed vowels to show that the action is being done to someone rather than by someone
Changed from past tense (عُرِفَ) to present tense (يُعْرَفْ) by adding يُ at the start and adjusting the vowels. Both are passive voice meaning 'to be known'
Added نَ at the end to show this applies to a group of women
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: and so not (they will not be harmed)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. In this ayah, it means they will not be harmed as a result of following the previous instruction.
Added يُ at the start to show someone is receiving the harm (passive voice), happening in present time
Added نَ at the end to show this refers to multiple females being harmed
Changed from basic root 'to be' into past tense 'he was' by adding the vowels
Adding و at beginning connects this word to what came before it in the ayah, showing this was part of Allah's attributes (غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا)
Changed to the specific name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of كَانَ - was)
steps.n: Added the ُ to show Allah is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah. Also added shadda to emphasize the L sound and changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects with previous word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
This form shows someone who does something a lot - here, someone who forgives again and again

steps.m: constantly forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds -an at the end to show this word is describing Allah in this sentence

steps.m: to show kindness and care for someone

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root letters into a complete verb meaning 'to show mercy'
Adding ي makes it describe someone who shows a lot of kindness all the time
Adding shadda makes the meaning even stronger

steps.m: extremely kind and caring (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding ًا (tanween fatha) at the end shows this word is being used as a description/adjective in the sentence
Starting with the basic conditional particle إِنْ which shows something depends on something else
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'

steps.m: if indeed (introducing a condition in يَنتَهِ)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل and changed إِ to ئِ to connect the letters better. This word introduces a condition for what follows in يَنتَهِ (they desist)
Basic negative word that makes the verb after it (يَنتَهِ - end/stop) mean 'did not' instead of 'does not'
The letter ل gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after another ل sound in لَّئِن
Added vowels to connect the letters and make past tense
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ت between first and second root letters to show the action is done to oneself

steps.m: he stops himself (in a conditional phrase)
steps.n: Removed final ي and added sukun because this word comes after لَّمْ in a conditional phrase

steps.m: to pass through secretly or escape

steps.n: Added alif between first and second letters to show doing something back and forth (like going between belief and disbelief)
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to show someone who does this action
Added ال to point to specific hypocrites being discussed
Added ون to make it plural (more than one hypocrite), with a small spelling change in the middle
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like adding 'the' or 'those' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. The ا in ال changes to ٱ after و

steps.m: in/within/inside (location word connecting to قُلُوبِهِم - their hearts)
steps.n: This is a primitive preposition (حرف جر) that has no derivational history - it exists in this basic form. Not derived from a root.

steps.m: in/within/inside (location word connecting to قُلُوبِهِم - their hearts)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) mark under ف to show how it's pronounced

steps.m: in/within/inside (location word connecting to قُلُوبِهِم - their hearts)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Changed the vowels to make it a noun. A heart is called this because it constantly turns over emotions and thoughts.
Changed the pattern to show many hearts by adding و and changing vowels to ُ

steps.m: their hearts (referring to the hypocrites and those with disease in their hearts)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show these hearts belong to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to be sick or have a disease

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'sickness' by adding the vowels a-a
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added tanween to show this is referring to a general sickness, specifically referring to the spiritual disease in the hearts of the hypocrites mentioned in this verse

steps.m: one who spreads harmful rumors (subject of لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ)
steps.n: The prefix مُ and the vowel pattern create an active participle - someone who does the action. This person is being threatened in the verse with لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ (We will surely incite you against them)
Changes from singular to plural masculine form

steps.m: the ones who spread harmful rumors
steps.n: Makes the word definite

steps.m: the ones who spread harmful rumors
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end to show it's in a certain grammatical position

steps.m: and the ones who spread harmful rumors (coordinated with الْمُنٰفِقُونَ)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous group mentioned (الْمُنٰفِقُونَ - the hypocrites). Both groups are being warned in this verse.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the hypocrites and those who have disease within their hearts.
Added basic vowels to create a noun meaning a place of settlement
Changed middle vowel to kasra and added ي and ة to create specific noun for city
Added ال to make it definite and kasra at end because it follows في (in/at) in the ayah
Changed initial alif to hamzat al-wasl to help pronunciation when starting the word
Changes to a present tense verb showing 'we' as the doers
Adds لَ at the beginning to show this will definitely happen
Adds نَّ at the end to make the meaning even stronger, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Adds كَ at the end to show who is being incited (you)
Basic preposition showing accompaniment or means. This connects to the verb لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ (We will surely incite you) in the ayah.

steps.m: with them (object of لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ - We will surely incite you)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this applies to multiple people (them). This word shows who will be the target of the incitement mentioned in لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ.

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with a clear time gap between them. In this ayah, it connects what will happen (they will not be your neighbors) to what was mentioned before.
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يُجَاوِرُونَكَ (they dwell near you) into its opposite meaning - 'they do not dwell near you'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
This form shows interaction between two parties - being neighbors to each other
Added alif to make it past tense - 'he' lived near someone
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
Added كَ to show who they are living near to
Added kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation
Added ها which refers back to المدينة (the city) mentioned earlier in the ayah
The alif is lengthened for emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning

This is the base form of the exception particle that excludes something from what came before it. In this ayah, it excludes قَلِيلًا (a little while) from the previous statement about not being neighbors.
Added alif at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word قَلِيلًا (a little while).
Adding vowels to show this describes a quality of being small/few
Added ي to make it an adjective describing how much of something there is

steps.m: a little while (describing how long they will stay)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how something happens. In this ayah, it describes how long people will stay - only a little while.

steps.m: to reject or push away from mercy

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past-tense verb
Changed to show who receives the action instead of who does it
Changed to plural form to show it happens to many people

steps.m: those who are completely rejected (from mercy)
steps.n: The doubled م adds extra emphasis to show complete rejection
This is a question word asking about location

steps.m: wherever, anywhere (followed by ثُقِفُوٓا۟ - they are found)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the word more general, changing 'where' to 'wherever'. This word connects to ثُقِفُوٓا۟ (they are found) in the ayah, showing that wherever these people are found, something will happen to them.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: they were found/caught (subjects of أُخِذُوا۟ وَقُتِّلُوا۟)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show this happened to a group of people. This word is connected to أُخِذُوا۟ (they were seized) and قُتِّلُوا۟ (they were killed) in the ayah, showing what happened to these people.
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened TO something rather than something doing it
Added وا to show this happened to multiple people. This word is connected to قُتِّلُوا۟ (they were killed) in the ayah, showing a sequence of actions done to them.
The doubled middle letter (ّ) shows this killing was done with intensity, violence or repeatedly. This intensified form is often used in the Quran to show severe punishment.
The word changes to passive voice, showing someone was killed rather than did the killing
The وا ending shows this happened to multiple people (masculine plural)

steps.m: and they were killed/slain violently (connected to أُخِذُوا)
steps.n: The وَ connects this to the previous phrase about them being seized, showing these punishments happened together
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: to slaughter/kill thoroughly (related to قُتِّلُوا۟ in ayah)
steps.n: The تَفْعِيل pattern creates a verbal noun emphasizing thoroughness or intensity of the action

steps.m: with thorough slaughter/killing (describes how قُتِّلُوا۟ happened)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this word describe how the action of قُتِّلُوا۟ (they were killed) happened in the ayah
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to show something that happens regularly
Added ة to show one specific instance of the practice
Added fatha at the end because this word is connected to 'Allah' (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, showing it's Allah's way
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one supreme deity. This makes it a proper name for God.
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else (سُنَّةِ - the way/practice). The alif at the start is written as a wasla (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects 'Allah's way' to 'those who' in the ayah.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly
Changed the ي to ى which is a common writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ to make it specific
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word describes the people who passed away (خَلَوْا۟) before.
Added fathas to make it past tense. The weak letter و changes to ا at the end of past tense verbs.
Added وْا to show multiple people did this action in the past. This word describes those who came before, relating to Allah's way (سُنَّةَ) with previous people.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'the way of Allah' to 'before', showing this way existed from before.
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'before' from the root concept of facing/coming before something
Added damma (U sound) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
This is a special word that makes strong negatives about the future, like saying 'absolutely will not' or 'never will'

steps.m: and never (will you find) (connects to تَجِدَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This word connects to تَجِدَ (you will find) to say 'and you will never find'
Basic root letters connected with vowels to make past tense 'he found'
Changed to present tense form. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start of a present tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Changed the final vowel to fatha because it comes after لَن which means 'will never' and requires this change

steps.m: to make or follow a clear path

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to make a path' into the noun 'a path'
Added ة to make it refer to one specific practice or way
Added kasra to show this is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ)
Added لِ (li) meaning 'for/to' at the start of the word
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the first letter merged with the ل to become one letter with a shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else. In this ayah, it shows this is Allah's way/practice (sunnah).
Added تَ at the beginning and ي after second root letter to make the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of replacing'

steps.m: any alteration/change (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is indefinite and is the object being found in the phrase 'you will not find'
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to match. This verb has الناس (the people) as its subject.
Added كَ at end to show you are being asked. The hamza moved position due to pronunciation rules.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Root means being social/friendly. Changed to mean 'people' since humans are social beings. The ن-ا-س pattern is used for this meaning.
Added ٱل to specify these particular people. Also added a shadda (ّ) on ن because it comes after ٱل.

steps.m: the people (who ask about the Hour)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the asking (يَسْـَٔلُكَ)
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows what something is about or concerning
Added vowel marks to show how the word connects to the words around it. This preposition connects 'they ask you' (يَسْـَٔلُكَ) with 'the Hour' (ٱلسَّاعَةِ) to mean 'they ask you about the Hour'

steps.m: to move or pass through time

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding an alif between first two letters and a fatha on each letter
Added ة to make it into a noun meaning one unit or instance of time passing
Added ال to make it refer specifically to the Day of Judgment, and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the Hour (object of عَنِ - about)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَنِ (about) which requires this ending
Changed the root into a command by adding a 'u' sound (damma) and making the middle letter silent (sukun)
The weak letter و is dropped in the command form. This is a common change with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي as their middle letter).
Added shadda and kasra to show this is an emphasis word that affects what comes after it
Added ما at the end which changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This word is modifying how people ask about the Hour, emphasizing that they ONLY ask you about it.
Changes the verb (action) into a noun (thing) by changing the vowel pattern
Adds ُ (damma) at the end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: its knowledge (referring to the Hour/Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adds هَا meaning 'its' to show we're talking about the knowledge of the Hour (السَّاعَةِ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Starting with the basic root letters that carry the meaning of being present or near
Added vowel marks to transform it into a preposition. In this ayah, it shows where the knowledge is located - with Allah.
Changed to the proper name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word belongs to عِندَ (with/at) in the phrase عِندَ ٱللَّهِ (with Allah)
Basic interrogative word meaning 'what'

steps.m: and what (connects to يُدْرِيكَ - makes you know)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this question word to the previous statement. This word introduces a question about what makes you know about the Hour.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing someone else to know
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added كَ to show you are the one being informed
This is a particle that expresses hope or possibility. It has no root since it's a basic building block of Arabic.

steps.m: perhaps/maybe (expressing hope that ٱلسَّاعَةَ/the Hour is near)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and shadda to make the word functional in the sentence. Here it introduces a hopeful possibility about the Hour (Day of Judgment) being near.
The root letters were connected with vowels, and the middle letter و changed to a long ا to show ongoing action
Added ة to make it a specific instance of time
Added ال (the) to make it specific, turning 'an hour' into 'THE Hour' - a special name for Judgment Day

steps.m: the Hour (object of يَسْـَٔلُكَ - they ask you about)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because it's the object being asked about in يَسْـَٔلُكَ (they ask you)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
تَ at start shows it's 'she' or 'you' doing the action in present time
Added vowels to connect the root letters together, making a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he came near' (a verb) to 'near' (a describing word) by using the pattern فَعِيل to show a quality. Like changing 'run' to 'fast'.

steps.m: near (describes how the Hour will be)
steps.n: The tanwin (ًا) shows this describes how something will be. Here it's telling us the Hour (ٱلسَّاعَةَ) will be near.
This is the basic form of the emphasis word that tells us to pay special attention to what comes next
Added the kasra (ِ) under the إ and shadda (ّ) on ن to show this is a strong emphasis word
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters. The first ل combines with the second ل to make a stronger sound (shown by ّ)

steps.m: Allah (as the object of لَعَنَ - cursed)
steps.n: Added final vowel mark to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb لَعَنَ (cursed). The hamza at the start is written as ٱ when connecting to the previous word.

steps.m: to reject, curse, or remove from mercy

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word comes after 'Indeed, Allah' (إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ) showing that Allah is the one who did the cursing.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from an action to describing someone who does that action
Changed from singular to plural to show many people
Added 'the' at the beginning to show these are specific disbelievers. The word is the object of لَعَنَ (cursed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added أ at start to show making something happen to something else. Also combined the double د with a shadda mark.
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is connected to سَعِيرًا (blazing fire) which shows what was prepared.
Added fatha to show this is connecting to something

steps.m: for them (refers to الْكٰفِرِينَ - the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them'. This 'them' refers back to 'the disbelievers' (الْكٰفِرِينَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic noun form
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show intensity

steps.m: an intensely blazing fire (object of أَعَدَّ)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being prepared in the phrase 'and He prepared for them a blazing fire'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action

steps.m: those who are staying/remaining (describing their state in فِيهَآ/in it)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural. This describes the state of people in the context of the ayah, showing how they are remaining in a place (فِيهَآ/in it)
Added ها ('ha') at the end to mean 'in it/her' instead of just 'in'
Changed the alif to a long alif (آ) for emphasis. This word refers back to Hell (جَهَنَّم) which was mentioned earlier.
Adding short 'a' sounds (fatha) to the root letters to make the basic noun meaning 'forever'

steps.m: forever (describing how long they will stay in it)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif makes this word describe how the staying (خٰلِدِينَ) happens - they will stay there forever
This is the basic negative word that means 'not' - it makes the words after it negative (like adding 'not' in English)
The shadda makes the 'l' sound stronger. This word negates يَجِدُونَ (they find), making it mean 'they do not find'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they find (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah who will not find any protector or helper in Hell.

steps.m: to protect, guard, be close to

steps.n: Added vowels (َ ِ َ) to make it mean 'he protected' in the past tense
Adding ّ (doubling mark) makes it mean someone who does a lot of protecting

steps.m: any protector/guardian (as object of لَّا يَجِدُونَ - they will not find)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being acted upon (what they won't find) and is indefinite (any protector)
Added fatha to show this is a connecting word
Added the negation particle لَا to create a compound meaning. This combination is used to add a negative statement to what came before.
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha
Forms pattern فَعِيل indicating someone who helps/gives victory intensively
The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find)
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
The doubled middle letter shows this action happens repeatedly or with intensity

steps.m: they are being turned over repeatedly (subject: وُجُوهُهُمْ - their faces)
steps.n: Changed from past tense to present tense by adding تُ at start and ُ at end. Now shows faces are being flipped over repeatedly (not someone doing the flipping)

steps.m: to face or turn towards something

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning the physical face
Makes the word plural to show many faces
Added damma to show this is the subject being turned over in the fire

steps.m: their faces (subject of تُقَلَّبُ - are turned over)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show these faces belong to them
This is a basic preposition that shows their faces are in the fire. It connects the word 'faces' (وُجُوهُهُمْ) to 'the fire' (ٱلنَّارِ)
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Root meaning 'light/heat' becomes the noun 'fire' by changing middle letter و to ا
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence
Added ال (the) to make it 'the fire' instead of just 'fire'
Added basic past tense form with fatha vowels. The middle و changes to ا in this pattern.
Changes from 'he said' (past) to 'he says' (present/future) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Changes from singular 'he says' to plural 'they say' by adding ون

steps.m: they are saying (referring to the disbelievers in the Fire)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is being done by a group. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers speaking while their faces are being turned in the Fire.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. لَيْتَ is a special word that expresses wishes or regret about something.
Added يٰ at the beginning to show strong emotion, like saying 'oh!' in English when you really wish something had happened differently.
Added نَآ at the end to show that 'we' are the ones expressing the wish. The long alif (آ) shows strong emotion.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
Added أَ at the start to show first person (I/we), and changed the middle ا to make it past tense I/we form

steps.m: we obeyed (subject of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. This word is part of what the people are saying in the Fire, as shown by يَقُولُونَ (they say).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to elongate the sound
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَطَعْنَا - we obeyed)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' comes from 'al-ilah' (the god), but became a unique name. Here it has a fatha mark to show Allah is being obeyed
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle و becomes ا in past tense.
Added أ at start and made word shorter to make a command (like 'Obey!')
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this with previous statement (they are saying 'we wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the messenger')
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added ٱل to show we're talking about a specific messenger
Added a special ending to show this messenger was the one being obeyed in the sentence
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the past tense pattern. This is common with words containing و or ي in the middle.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (for a group of people)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Doubling the middle letter ب shows this is someone who cares for others perfectly and completely
Added نَا at the end to show this is our Lord. This word is being acted upon by the verb 'they said' (قَالُوا) in this ayah.
Added the long alif (آ) at the end to show this is at the end of a phrase or sentence
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English

steps.m: indeed we (subject of أَطَعْنَا - we obeyed)
steps.n: Adding آ at the end means 'we'. This word connects to أَطَعْنَا (we obeyed) in this ayah, showing who did the obeying.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made (someone) obey / caused to obey
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes it from 'to obey' to 'to make someone obey'
Changed from past tense 'he obeyed' to a command 'obey!' by dropping the long 'aa' sound

steps.m: we obeyed (سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَآءَنَا - our leaders and our great ones)
steps.n: Added نَا to show 'we' did the action. This word refers to the disbelievers speaking about how they obeyed their leaders who led them astray.
Adding ة at the end makes this a singular noun for someone who leads or is in charge.

steps.m: our leaders/masters (acted on by أَطَعْنَا - we obeyed)
steps.n: Adding نَا at the end shows these are our leaders. The ة changes to ت when we add نَا. This word is being acted on by the verb 'we obeyed' (أَطَعْنَا).
Changes from verb 'to be big' into adjective 'big/great'
Changed the word pattern to make it plural, showing many great ones
Added a mark to show this word is being acted upon by the verb 'we obeyed' (أطعنا)
Added نا to show these great ones belong to us (the speakers)

steps.m: and our great ones (being acted upon by أطعنا - we obeyed)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous one
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at the start to mean 'made someone do something' - changed from 'lost way' to 'made someone lose way'. The ل is doubled to show extra strength.
Adding وا makes it plural for 'they'

steps.m: they made us lose our way
steps.n: Adding نا at the end changes 'they led astray' to 'they led us astray' - we became the ones being misled. The word comes after 'they said'.

steps.m: so they made us lose our way (responding to وَقَالُوا - and they said)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this is their response or explanation for what they said before

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added 'the' at the beginning. The ل in ال combines with س to make a stronger س sound (written with ّ)
Adds accusative case marker alif with fatha

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture with love
Lord (one who nurtures with complete care)
steps.n: The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes this mean someone who provides perfect care and nurturing
Added نَا to show we're talking about our Lord, like saying 'my book' but for a group
The extra alif shows we're calling out to our Lord, like saying 'O our Lord!'
Changed root letters and added vowels to make past tense 'he brought'
Changed the last vowel from 'a' to 'i' to make it a command to one person

steps.m: bring to them! (command to our Lord)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show who receives the action. This is a command to Allah (رَبَّنَآ) to bring punishment to the disbelievers.

steps.m: to make something double/multiple of what it was

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning one double portion or multiple of something

steps.m: two double portions (of الْعَذَابِ - the punishment)
steps.n: The ين ending here specifically means exactly two portions. This word relates to الْعَذَابِ (the punishment) that comes after it, showing they will get double portions of punishment.
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْعَذَابِ (the punishment). This combination means 'from the punishment'
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this verb into a noun - like how 'punish' becomes 'punishment' in English
Added ال at the beginning to mean 'the' and ِ at the end because it follows the word مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and added alif to make it a command (like 'curse!' in English). This command is directed at Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the previous request in the ayah (رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِهِمْ - Our Lord, give them)
Added هُمْ (them) to show who should be cursed. This refers to the same people mentioned in the previous request.

steps.m: to curse or remove from mercy

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb 'he cursed' by adding vowel marks
Changed the middle vowel to a sukun to make it a noun instead of a verb

steps.m: a curse (emphasizing the curse in وَٱلْعَنْهُمْ)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this is emphasizing the curse mentioned in the verb وَٱلْعَنْهُمْ (curse them)
Added vowels and ي to make it describe something that has this quality
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing the curse (لَعْنًا) mentioned in the verse. The ً marks (called tanween) show we say 'a' before the word in English.

steps.m: to call attention, to point out

steps.n: The middle letter is doubled with shadda to emphasize the call for attention
Adding يٰٓ at the beginning makes it a stronger call for attention, like shouting 'Hey!' to get someone's attention
Adding ها makes it clear you're calling to multiple people, like saying 'O you all!'

steps.m: O you all! (addressing the believers - الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا)
steps.n: The damma (ُ) shows this is directly addressing a group. This word is calling out to the believers (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) who are mentioned right after it.
This is the basic form of the word used to describe someone or something
Changed from singular to plural by adding ين at the end
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like adding 'the' or 'those' in English

steps.m: those who (describing the believers who are being addressed)
steps.n: Added final vowel marks and changed spelling slightly (ا to ٱ) to match Quranic writing style. This word describes the believers being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا (O you who)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The hamza and alif at the start make it mean 'to make oneself feel safe by believing'. This word is part of a common phrase 'O you who believe' that starts many verses in the Quran.

steps.m: they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: The suffix وا indicates 'they' (masculine plural) as the subject
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. Here it negates the verb تَكُونُوا۟ (be), changing its meaning to 'do not be'
Added fatha mark (َ) on ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Added damma and sukun to connect the letters in their basic form
Adding تَ at the start shows 'you' are doing the action. This word is addressing the believers (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Adding وا makes it plural masculine 'you all'
This is a special word used to point to people or things we're talking about. The ال at the beginning makes it specific.

steps.m: like those who (connected to تَكُونُوا۟ - be not)
steps.n: Added ك at the beginning to show comparison. This word connects to تَكُونُوا۟ (be not) in the ayah, telling believers not to be like certain people.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي becomes ى when it has a fatha.
Adding آ at start changes meaning to 'cause to harm'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The ى changes to و before this ending.

This is a proper noun of non-Arabic origin (borrowed from Hebrew/Ancient Egyptian). It doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns. The name possibly comes from Egyptian meaning 'born of' or 'child of', though some suggest it means 'drawn out of water' in Hebrew.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the name
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ى to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound at the end
Doubling the middle letter (shadda) makes the meaning causative/intensive

steps.m: and so he cleared (someone) from blame
steps.n: ف means 'and then' or 'so'

steps.m: and so he cleared him from blame (referring to موسى)
steps.n: Adds 'him' (هُ) to the end to show who was cleared from blame (referring to Moses)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'deity' (إِلَه) became the name 'Allah' by adding ال and some special changes to the letters. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing something in this sentence.
Connected the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together
The ن changes to م and gets a shadda because it's easier to say when the words are connected - this is like how 'in my' becomes 'immy' in casual English
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added vowels to show past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: and he was (connects this phrase to the previous part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah
Added vowels to make it into a word meaning 'near' or 'with'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show something is 'with Allah'.
Added ال and merged it with the word, changing إ to ل to create the unique name Allah
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and added shadda on ل to emphasize it. The hamza at the start becomes a waṣla (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.
Added vowels and ي to change from 'face/turn' to mean 'someone others turn to with respect'
Added tanween alif to show this describes Moses in the sentence. This word tells us about Moses's status with Allah.
Adds يَا ('O') at the start to call out to someone, like saying 'Hey you!'
Adds ها to make it a complete way to address someone
We start with the basic word ذا meaning 'this' and change it to ذي which can mean 'who'
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it talk about many people instead of just one person
Added the correct spelling and vowel marks. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) to mean 'those who believed'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the first letter to make the meaning stronger - like saying someone actively chooses to trust/believe rather than just feeling safe

steps.m: they believed (those who are addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people are doing this. This word is describing the people being addressed at the start of the verse with يٰٓأَيُّهَا (O you who...)

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to make it mean 'protect oneself' instead of 'protect others'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Command form of 'protect/fear' - telling a group to be mindful of Allah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is being addressed)
steps.n: This is the unique name of God in Islam, formed by adding ال (the) and changing the word slightly. The َ at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence.

steps.m: to say something, to speak words

steps.n: Changed from basic root 'to say' into a command by adding marks - like telling someone 'Say!'
Adding وَ (and) at the beginning to connect with the previous command اتَّقُوا (fear/be mindful of) in the ayah

steps.m: and (all of you) say! (command to group)
steps.n: Adding وا at the end to show this command is for multiple people (matching 'O you who believe' at start of ayah)

steps.m: to say something, to speak words

steps.n: This pattern turns the action of saying into the thing that is said (like changing 'speak' to 'speech')
Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being commanded to be said. It's like the 'what' in 'say what?' - the thing that should be said.
Added vowels and ي to make it describe something that has this quality
Added ًا to show this word is describing the word قَوْلًا (speech) in the sentence
Added sukun on first letter to prepare for adding prefix
Added يُ at start to show present/future tense, changed middle vowel to kasra to show the action affects something else, and added sukun at end since this is in the middle of a sentence
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word is receiving the action of يُصْلِحْ (he rectifies/corrects) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a single action to many actions by making it plural - adds أَ at start and ا after م

steps.m: your deeds (referring to the people being addressed in يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ - He will correct for you)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show the deeds belong to the people being spoken to. This word is connected to the previous phrase 'He will correct for you'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he forgives (in response to وَمَن يُطِعِ - whoever obeys)
steps.n: The sukun at the end shows this is a response to a condition - Allah will forgive whoever obeys Him

steps.m: and He forgives (in response to وَمَن يُطِعِ - whoever obeys)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous phrase about Allah improving their deeds

steps.m: for/to (connected to يُصْلِحْ - he will rectify)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this is connected to what comes after it. This particle shows something is being done for someone's benefit.

steps.m: for you all (object of يُصْلِحْ - he will rectify)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people being spoken to. The ُ (damma) shows this is for multiple people, and the ْ (sukun) shows the word is complete.

steps.m: to sin or make a mistake

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form for a single sin
Changed vowels and added و to make it plural, showing there are many sins

steps.m: your sins (object of يَغْفِرْ - He will forgive)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show the sins belong to the people being addressed. This word is what is being forgiven in the phrase 'He will forgive your sins'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - whoever does something will get a result
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This introduces the person who will obey Allah and His messenger.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense form. The و changes to ا in this pattern.
Added يُ at the start to show present tense. The middle letter changes from ا to ي in present tense.
Added kasra at the end to show it's in jussive mood (like after لَمْ)
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in يُطِعِ)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being obeyed in the phrase 'whoever obeys Allah'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to damma and added و after it to show someone who does this action as their role
Added هُ at the end to mean 'His' (referring to Allah). The fatha before it shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يُطِعِ (obeys).
Added conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning of the word
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is the result of the previous condition (whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he has achieved success (subject of فَقَدْ)
steps.n: The middle weak letter و changes to ا in the past tense. This word follows فَقَدْ (then certainly) in this ayah.
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the result of winning rather than the action of winning.

steps.m: a great victory/success (object of فَازَ - 'has achieved')
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes what kind of victory was achieved. This word is the object that receives the action in فَازَ (has achieved) from the previous ayah.
Added ي between ظ and م, with َ on ع and ِ on ظ to show this describes something with a strong quality of greatness
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing the achievement (فَوْزًا) - like saying 'a great victory'
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. It's used at the start of a sentence to emphasize what comes next, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next word عَرَضْنَا (we offered) where we are offering the trust.
Added fatha (َ) after each letter to make it mean 'he presented' in the past tense
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word follows إِنَّا (indeed we) at the start of the verse, emphasizing that Allah is speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'.

Added ا after second letter to form noun pattern فَعَال, with fatha vowels on first two consonants. This word is in accusative case because it is the direct object (thing being offered) of the verb عَرَضْنَا (we offered).

steps.m: a trust/something entrusted (object of عَرَضْنَا - we offered)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ة to make this a specific instance of trust/security that can be given to someone

steps.m: the trust (object of عَرَضْنَا - we offered)
steps.n: Added definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: the trust (object of عَرَضْنَا - we offered)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word receives the action of the verb 'we offered'

This is a preposition that shows the offer (عَرَضْنَا) was made to or directed upon the heavens (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ). In this ayah, it helps explain that the trust was offered to the heavens, earth, and mountains.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding a long 'aa' sound and hamza at the end
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Added kasra at the end because it comes after عَرَضْنَا (we presented/offered). The alifs are written as long alifs (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun
Added ال to make it specific
Added و to connect it to the previous word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens)

steps.m: and the earth (object of عَرَضْنَا/we offered)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it's connected to عَرَضْنَا (we offered) in the ayah
Changes from single mountain to many mountains
Adds 'and' to connect this word with previous words
Adding -i sound at the end to show it belongs to/connects with another word
Adding definite article to make the noun definite
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-a
Changed the ending and added نَ to show many women did the action instead of one man

steps.m: and so they (feminine) refused (responding to عَرَضْنَا - We offered)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of the previous action (the offering mentioned earlier in the verse)
This is a particle that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces what was refused - carrying the trust. The word after it (يَحْمِلْنَهَا) shows what they refused to do.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to feminine plural by adding نَ and removing final ُ
Added هَا to show what is being carried (referring back to الْأَمَانَةَ - the trust)

steps.m: to be anxiously fearful and concerned

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause' the original action
Added نَ at the end to show multiple females felt fear (like 'they' instead of 'he')

steps.m: and they (feminine) became anxiously fearful (of الْأَمَانَةَ - the trust)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this action to the previous events in the verse about offering the trust
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The sukun (ْ) is added to show where the word pauses.
Added ها which refers back to 'the trust' (الْأَمَانَةَ) mentioned earlier in the verse. We use the feminine form because 'trust' is feminine in Arabic.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what the human (الْإِنسٰنُ) did in the story.

steps.m: he carried it (referring to الْأَمَانَةَ - the trust)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what was carried - referring back to the trust (الْأَمَانَةَ) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: and he carried it (referring to الْأَمَانَةَ - the trust)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous actions in the verse

steps.m: to be close and familiar with others

steps.n: Root means 'to be social' - changed to mean 'human' since humans are social beings
Added ُ to show this is the doer in the sentence
Added ٱلْ to specify this particular human being mentioned in the ayah who carried the trust
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed/verily'. This emphasizes what comes after it.
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being talked about (referring to الْإِنسٰنُ - mankind/human - from the previous ayah)
Added the small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he was (describing the human in الْإِنسٰنُ)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels. This is a common change in Arabic that makes words easier to say.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added و after second letter and changed vowels to show someone who does this action a lot

steps.m: one who often acts unfairly (describing كَانَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word describes the subject of كَانَ (was)

steps.m: to not know or understand something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added و after second root letter and changed vowels to show someone who has this quality very strongly
Added tanween alif to show this word describes the state of being in كَانَ (was)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something else happens
The ل (laam) merged with the following letter, shown by the shadda, but meaning stays same

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific deity
The U sound at the end shows this word is the subject
Added alif after first root letter to show pretending or faking an action. This creates the meaning of someone who shows one thing on the outside while hiding something different inside.
Added مُ at beginning and changed vowels to show someone who does this action as a habit
Added ال to point to specific hypocrites mentioned in the verse

steps.m: the hypocrites (those being punished by يُعَذِّبَ)
steps.n: Added ين to show multiple hypocrites. This word is receiving the action of punishment (يُعَذِّبَ) in this verse.
Added alif to show ongoing or repeated action of being false/hypocritical
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show the person who does the action
Added ات to make it feminine plural
Added ال to make it definite

steps.m: and the female hypocrites (acted upon by يُعَذِّبَ)
steps.n: Added و for 'and', changed vowel at end to show this word receives the action of punishment (يُعَذِّبَ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The م prefix turns the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
The ين suffix makes this a masculine plural noun in accusative case. This word is the object (the ones receiving the action) of the verb يُعَذِّبَ (to punish).

steps.m: the polytheists (object of يُعَذِّبَ - to punish)
steps.n: The word gets 'the' (ال) added at the start to make it specific polytheists
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: Adds مُ prefix to make it mean 'someone who does the action' (joins partners with Allah)
Changed from singular male form to plural female form by adding ات at the end. Shows we're talking about multiple women who worship false gods.
Adding ال makes it refer to specific female polytheists being punished (connected to يُعَذِّبَ - to punish)
Adding conjunction to connect with previous phrase
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The ي with fatha (يَ) at the start shows this is happening now or in the future. This verb is connected to يُعَذِّبَ (punish) from earlier in the verse, showing both actions happening together.

steps.m: and he returns back (subject of يُعَذِّبَ)
steps.n: Adds و with fatha (وَ) to connect this with the previous action of punishment, showing both purposes of Allah's action
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'a deity'
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
This is a basic preposition showing that something is on top of or directed towards something else. Here it connects how Allah turns in mercy upon/towards the believers.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word - 'a' sound (َ) after ع and after ل

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, or believe

steps.n: Adds مُ at the start to show who does the action (believer) and changes middle vowel to ِ
Forms regular masculine plural
Adds ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb يُعَذِّبَ (to punish)

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, or believe

steps.n: Root أمن becomes مؤمن by adding مُ at start to mean 'one who believes/trusts'
Added feminine plural ending ات
Added the definite article ال to make the word definite

steps.m: and the female believers (object of يُعَذِّبَ)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) at start, and kasra at end because it's receiving the action of punishing in this ayah
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it 'he was' in past tense
Added alif after first letter because of weak middle letter و

steps.m: and he was (connects to غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًۢا - forgiving and merciful)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to what comes after it, showing Allah was forgiving and merciful
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of يُعَذِّبَ - will punish)
steps.n: Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the unique name of the one true God. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing the action in this ayah - specifically the punishing mentioned in يُعَذِّبَ.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
This form shows someone who does something a lot - here, someone who forgives again and again

steps.m: constantly forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The word got an -an ending (tanween) to show it's describing Allah in this sentence
Adding vowels makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to show mercy'
Changed from a past-tense verb (he showed mercy) to a strong quality (very merciful) by adding ي in the middle

steps.m: extremely merciful (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed ending to show it describes Allah, and added emphasis with shadda (double letter) at start
